rules.rule_name: Sun in Aries Navamsa aspected by malefic leads to poverty

check_logic.condition: Sun is in Aries Navamsa AND Sun is aspected by a malefic planet
check_logic.calculation: 1. Determine the Sun's position in the Navamsa (D9) chart. 2. Check if the Sun is in Aries in D9. 3. Identify malefic planets (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Rahu, Ketu, debilitated planets, functional malefics based on Lagna). 4. Check if any identified malefic planet aspects the Sun in the Rasi chart (or D9 if specified by other texts, but typically Rasi aspects are considered for general effects unless D9 aspect is explicitly mentioned). Assuming Rasi aspect for general malefic influence.

rules.impact_if_violated: Leads to poverty (निर्धनः)
rules.extraction_notes: Specific planetary placement in Navamsa combined with malefic aspect leading to financial hardship.

rules.rule_name: Venus in Virgo Navamsa leads to beggary

check_logic.condition: Venus is in Virgo Navamsa
check_logic.calculation: Determine the Venus's position in the Navamsa (D9) chart. Check if Venus is in Virgo in D9.

rules.impact_if_violated: Leads to beggary (भिक्षुकः)
rules.extraction_notes: Specific planetary placement in Navamsa leading to extreme financial hardship.

rules.rule_name: Sun in debilitation sign and debilitation navamsa leads to loss of wealth, sons, and wife

check_logic.condition: Sun is in its debilitation sign (Libra) AND Sun is in its debilitation Navamsa (Libra)
check_logic.calculation: 1. Determine the Sun's Rasi position. Check if it is in Libra. 2. Determine the Sun's Navamsa (D9) position. Check if it is in Libra. Both conditions must be met.

rules.impact_if_violated: Loss of wealth, sons, and wife (विगतश्रीपुत्रदाराशनः), even if born in a royal family.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule emphasizes the severe impact of a deeply debilitated Sun, indicated by debilitation in both Rasi and Navamsa ('नीचर्थे त्वतिनीचभागसहिते' - in debilitation sign and accompanied by the most debilitated part).

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity Assessment (Debilitation & Inimical Sign)

check_logic.condition: For every planet in the natal chart (D1) and relevant divisional charts (Amsas).
check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign occupied by each planet in the Rashi chart (D1). Determine if the planet is in its sign of debilitation (depression) or an inimical sign based on classical dignity tables. Repeat this assessment for the planet's position in relevant divisional charts (e.g., Navamsa, Dashamsha), as implied by 'portions of them'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to correctly assess planetary dignity will lead to fundamental errors in understanding a planet's inherent strength, functional capacity, and its ability to deliver auspicious or inauspicious results, thereby invalidating subsequent interpretations and predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is inferred from the description of a severe outcome when 'all planets' are in their 'depression or inimical signs or portions of them'. This implies that checking these states for individual planets in both Rashi and Amsa charts is a foundational step in astrological analysis. 'Portions of them' refers to divisional charts (Amsas).

rules.rule_name: Rajayoga Destruction due to Weak Moon and Unoccupied Lagna

check_logic.condition: When a Rajayoga is indicated in the birth chart.
check_logic.calculation:
Check if ALL of the following conditions are met:
1. Moon's position meets ANY of the following criteria: (a) In the close of a moveable sign OR (b) In the beginning of a fixed sign OR (c) In the middle of a dual Rasi.
2. Moon's strength: Moon is without strength (Balavarjite).
3. Lagna occupation: The Lagna (ascendant) is unoccupied by any planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rajayoga, even if formed, will be destroyed or will not yield its promised results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 10
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies a set of conditions that, when simultaneously present, nullify or destroy an otherwise formed Rajayoga. The exact degree ranges for 'close', 'beginning', and 'middle' of signs for the Moon's position, and the precise definition of 'without strength' (Balavarjite) for the Moon, would require further classical interpretation or established computational methods.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter with Rahu/Ketu aspected by malefics

check_logic.condition: Jupiter is in conjunction with Rahu OR Ketu AND Jupiter is aspected by one or more malefic planets.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Jupiter's conjunctions and aspects. Identify malefic planets (typically Saturn, Mars, Sun, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon).

rules.impact_if_violated: The person born will do base acts.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'When Jupiter in conjunction with Rahu or Ketu is aspected by malefic planets, the person born will do base acts.'

rules.rule_name: Debilitated Jupiter aspected by another debilitated planet

check_logic.condition: Jupiter is in its sign of debilitation AND Jupiter is aspected by a planet that is also in its sign of debilitation.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Jupiter's sign to check for debilitation. Identify planets aspecting Jupiter and check their dignity (debilitation).

rules.impact_if_violated: Even a person born a Brahmin will commit wicked crimes.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'When Jupiter in his depression is aspected by a planet which is also in his depression sign, even a person that is born a Brahmin will commit wicked crimes.'

rules.rule_name: Moon & Lagna Lord conjunct Sun, aspected by Saturn

check_logic.condition: The Moon and the Lord of the Ascendant (Lagna Lord) are in conjunction with the Sun AND this conjunction (Moon, Lagna Lord, Sun) is aspected by Saturn.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Lagna Lord. Check for conjunctions of Moon, Lagna Lord, and Sun. Check for Saturn's aspect on this conjunction.

rules.impact_if_violated: The person born will be slothful.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'If the Moon and the lord of the rising sign be in conjunction with the Sun and aspected by Saturn, the person born will be slothful.'

rules.rule_name: Malefics in 10th house without benefic aspect

check_logic.condition: One or more malefic planets occupy the 10th house (Bhava) AND the 10th house (or the planets within it) is NOT aspected by any benefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets in the 10th house. Determine if they are malefic. Check for aspects from benefic planets (typically Jupiter, Venus, Mercury, waxing Moon) on the 10th house or its occupants.

rules.impact_if_violated: The person born will be a menial.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'If malefic planets occupy the 10th bhava and be without benefic aspect, the person born will be a menial.'

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and nature of any planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is exalted, debilitated, in its own sign, friendly sign, or inimical sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary strength and influence, leading to erroneous predictions regarding a planet's ability to deliver results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The text mentions 'all planets in debilitation or inimical signs' (सर्वग्रहेर्नीत्रसपत्लभौगः) as a condition for a specific outcome, implying that assessing planetary dignity is a fundamental and non-negotiable check. The verse is attributed to Sarvartha Chintamani within Jataka Parijata.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Combustion Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and influence of any planet other than the Sun.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a planet is within specific degrees of conjunction with the Sun, leading to combustion (Asta). The exact degree thresholds are not provided in this specific verse but are a standard classical concept.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to account for combustion will lead to an overestimation of the combust planet's strength and an incorrect assessment of its ability to deliver results, often rendering it weak or ineffective.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: The phrase 'eclipsed by the Sun' (अस्तङ्गतावन्त्यगे) directly refers to planetary combustion (Asta), a critical planetary state that must be checked as it significantly alters a planet's functional capacity. The verse is attributed to Sarvartha Chintamani within Jataka Parijata.

rules.rule_name: Paparksha Definition (Malefic-owned Sign)

check_logic.condition: When identifying signs owned by malefic planets (Paparksha) for astrological analysis.
check_logic.calculation:
A sign is classified as a Paparksha if it is owned by any of the natural malefic planets: Mars, Sun, or Saturn. 
- Mars owns Mesha (Aries) and Vrischika (Scorpio).
- Sun owns Simha (Leo).
- Saturn owns Makara (Capricorn) and Kumbha (Aquarius).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of malefic-owned signs will lead to misinterpretation of yogas, planetary dignities, and functional malefic/benefic classifications, thereby invalidating subsequent astrological analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the clarification distinguishing 'Paparksha' from 'Krurarksha'. This rule defines the broader category of signs owned by natural malefics.

rules.rule_name: Krurarksha Definition (Odd Malefic Sign)

check_logic.condition: When identifying 'Krura' signs, specifically for yogas where this term is used (e.g., 'कर (Krura) as applied to a house').
check_logic.calculation:
A sign is classified as a Krurarksha if it is an *odd* sign owned by a natural malefic planet (Mars, Sun, or Saturn). 
- Mars owns Mesha (Aries - odd).
- Sun owns Simha (Leo - odd).
- Saturn owns Kumbha (Aquarius - odd).
(Vrischika and Makara are excluded as they are even signs, despite being owned by malefics).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of Krura signs will lead to misinterpretation of specific yogas that utilize this precise classification, leading to erroneous predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the clarification distinguishing 'Paparksha' from 'Krurarksha'. This rule defines the narrower category of 'Krura' signs as only the *odd* signs owned by natural malefics.

rules.rule_name: Impact of Planet at Extreme Debilitation Point

check_logic.condition: A planet is in its sign of debilitation.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet's longitude is at its extreme debilitation point (Parama Neecha).

rules.impact_if_violated: Severe negative impact on the significations of the planet and the houses it rules/occupies, potentially leading to loss of fortune, family, and livelihood.
rules.extraction_notes: General principle derived from the example of Sun at extreme depression point, implying it applies to any planet.

rules.rule_name: Dignity Nullified by Extreme Debilitation

check_logic.condition: A planet is in its own Trikona, exaltation, or own house.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the same planet is simultaneously at its extreme debilitation point (Parama Neecha).

rules.impact_if_violated: The expected positive results from the planet's strong dignity (own Trikona, exaltation, own house) will not manifest.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a direct quote from Saravali within the notes of Jataka Parijata Sloka 2.

rules.rule_name: Debilitation Effects Require Bhava Confirmation

check_logic.condition: A planet is in its debilitation sign (Neecha Kshetra) or debilitated Navamsa (Neecha Navamsa).
check_logic.calculation: Confirm the planet's placement in a specific bhava. The text implies that the negative effects of debilitation are not automatic and require a specific bhava placement to manifest. (The text does not specify *which* bhava, but the principle of requiring bhava confirmation is the rule).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the impact of debilitated planets if bhava placement is not considered. Negative effects may not manifest if the bhava condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule highlights that debilitation in Rashi or Navamsa alone is insufficient for negative effects; bhava placement is a necessary condition. The specific bhava is not mentioned, but the principle is clear.

rules.rule_name: Benefics in Dussthanas destroy Rajayoga

check_logic.condition: Natural benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, unafflicted Mercury) occupy the 6th, 8th, or 12th houses from the Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify natural benefics in the chart. 2. Check the house placement of each benefic. 3. If any benefic is placed in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Destroys or significantly weakens the Rajayoga potential of the horoscope.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a condition that destroys Rajayoga, comparing its effect to planets in debilitation, inimical signs, and malefic Shashtiamshas.

rules.rule_name: Planets in Debilitation, Inimical Sign, and Malefic Shashtiamsha destroy Rajayoga

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies its sign of debilitation AND an inimical sign (to that planet) AND a malefic 60th portion (Shashtiamsha).
check_logic.calculation: For each planet: 1. Determine if the planet is in its sign of debilitation. 2. Determine if the sign occupied by the planet is inimical to it (based on planetary friendships). 3. Determine the nature (malefic/benefic) of the Shashtiamsha (D60) occupied by the planet. If all three conditions are met for any planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Destroys or significantly weakens the Rajayoga potential of the horoscope.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a condition that destroys Rajayoga, stating its effect is 'no less than' benefics in Dussthanas.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Debilitation - Venus

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dignity and fundamental strength of Venus
check_logic.calculation: Check if Venus is placed in the sign of Virgo.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Venus's fundamental strength and ability to give results, leading to erroneous predictions regarding relationships, luxury, wealth, and artistic pursuits.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 13
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'नीचे भृगौ' (Venus in its depression sign). The specific sign for Venus's debilitation (Virgo) is a standard astrological fact implied by this phrase.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Venus in Saturn's Navamsa

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dignity of Venus in divisional charts, particularly Navamsa (D9)
check_logic.calculation: Check if Venus is placed in a Navamsa ruled by Saturn (Capricorn Navamsa or Aquarius Navamsa).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Venus's strength and ability to give results in the D9 chart, affecting predictions related to marriage, partnerships, and inner qualities.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 13
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'मन्दनवांशके वा' (or in a Navamsa owned by Saturn). This refers to Venus being in a Navamsa of Capricorn or Aquarius, which are Saturn's signs.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Affliction - Dusthana Placement with Malefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and affliction of any planet
check_logic.calculation: Check if a planet is placed in a Dusthana (6th, 8th, or 12th house) AND is aspected by a natural malefic planet (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Sun, Rahu, Ketu, or a functionally malefic planet).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify significant affliction to a planet, leading to an overestimation of its benefic results or an underestimation of challenges it may bring in its dasha/bhukti.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 13
rules.extraction_notes: Generalized from the specific context of Venus being in a Dusthana and aspected by Saturn ('दुःस्थानगे भानुसुतेक्षिते च') to a general principle of planetary affliction. Dusthanas are 6, 8, 12. Saturn is a natural malefic.

rules.rule_name: Lagnesha Strength Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall chart strength or specific yogas involving the Lagna Lord.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the Shadbala, Avasthas, and general dignity of the Lagna Lord. The text implies a state of 'no strength', which means very low Shadbala or poor avasthas.

rules.impact_if_violated: A Lagna Lord with no strength indicates general weakness in the native's constitution, health, and overall life path, leading to struggles and lack of vitality. It is a necessary condition for the Rekha Yoga.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This is a condition for the Rekha Yoga. 'No strength' is a qualitative assessment, implying very low Shadbala or poor avasthas.

rules.rule_name: Lagnesha Aspect by 8th Lord

check_logic.condition: When assessing the condition and influences on the Lagna Lord.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the lord of the 8th house from the Lagna. Determine if this 8th lord aspects the Lagna Lord using standard planetary aspects (e.g., Jupiter's 5th, 7th, 9th; Mars' 4th, 7th, 8th; Saturn's 3rd, 7th, 10th; others 7th).

rules.impact_if_violated: Malefic influence on the Lagna Lord by the 8th house lord indicates challenges, obstacles, health issues, longevity concerns, and potential for sudden events or transformations in the native's life. It is a necessary condition for the Rekha Yoga.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This is a condition for the Rekha Yoga. The 8th lord is generally considered a malefic influence on the Lagna Lord.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter Combustion

check_logic.condition: When assessing Jupiter's strength, dignity, and influence in the chart.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the angular distance between Jupiter and the Sun. If Jupiter is within the specified orb of combustion for Jupiter (typically 11 degrees), it is considered combust.

rules.impact_if_violated: Combustion significantly weakens Jupiter's significations, potentially leading to issues related to wisdom, wealth, children, dharma, good fortune, and spiritual guidance. It is a necessary condition for the Rekha Yoga.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Jupiter is retrograde while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced or nullified.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Jupiter is in its own sign or exaltation while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This is a condition for the Rekha Yoga. 'Obscured by the Sun' is a direct reference to combustion. The standard combustion orb for Jupiter is 11 degrees.

rules.rule_name: Rajayoga Annihilation by Malefics in Kendras

check_logic.condition: When a Rajayoga is identified in the birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if ALL natural malefic planets (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu) are simultaneously: 1. Placed in a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th). 2. In their sign of debilitation OR an inimical sign. 3. Aspected by natural benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, Mercury if unafflicted, Moon if strong). 4. These aspecting natural benefic planets are themselves situated in the 6th, 8th, or 12th houses.

rules.impact_if_violated: Any Rajayoga present in the chart is annihilated or significantly weakened.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific planetary configuration that annihilates Rajayogas. The provided English translation was incomplete, so a more complete interpretation of the Sanskrit verse was used. The verse also mentions 'नीचारातिक्रूरषष्ठयंशकाश्च' (and in debilitated, inimical, cruel Shashtyamshas) as an additional reinforcing factor for the malefic planets, further emphasizing the annihilation effect, but it is not included as a strict requirement for the primary annihilation condition.

rules.rule_name: Rekayoga Condition: Benefic planets in Dusthanas

check_logic.condition: Benefic planets are placed in the 6th, 8th, and 12th bhavas.
check_logic.calculation: Identify natural benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury when unafflicted). Check their house placements. This condition requires benefics to be in all three dusthanas or a significant number of them.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rekayoga is not formed or its injurious effects are significantly reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: One of the conditions for the formation of Rekayoga.

rules.rule_name: Rekayoga Condition: Malefic planets in Kendras and Trikonas

check_logic.condition: Malefic planets occupy Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) and Trikona (1st, 5th, 9th) positions.
check_logic.calculation: Identify natural malefic planets (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury when afflicted). Check their house placements in Kendras and Trikonas.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rekayoga is not formed or its injurious effects are significantly reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: One of the conditions for the formation of Rekayoga.

rules.rule_name: Rekayoga Condition: 11th Lord without strength

check_logic.condition: The lord of the 11th bhava is without strength.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the 11th house lord using Shadbala or other classical strength metrics. 'Without strength' implies a significant lack of strength, falling below a generally accepted minimum threshold for planetary strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rekayoga is not formed or its injurious effects are significantly reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: One of the conditions for the formation of Rekayoga. The text does not specify a numerical threshold for 'without strength'.

rules.rule_name: Rekayoga Condition: Lagna Lord conjunct Malefic

check_logic.condition: The lord of the rising sign (Lagna lord) is in conjunction with a malefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Lagna lord. Check for conjunction (within a defined orb, typically 6-10 degrees) with any natural malefic planet (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury when afflicted).

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rekayoga is not formed or its injurious effects are significantly reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: One of the conditions for the formation of Rekayoga.

rules.rule_name: Rekayoga Condition: Venus is Combust

check_logic.condition: Venus has become invisible (combust).
check_logic.calculation: Check if Venus is within the combustion orb of the Sun (typically 10 degrees).

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rekayoga is not formed or its injurious effects are significantly reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: One of the conditions for the formation of Rekayoga. 'Invisible' refers to combustion.

rules.rule_name: Rekayoga Condition: Jupiter is Combust

check_logic.condition: Jupiter has become invisible (combust).
check_logic.calculation: Check if Jupiter is within the combustion orb of the Sun (typically 11 degrees).

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rekayoga is not formed or its injurious effects are significantly reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: One of the conditions for the formation of Rekayoga. 'Invisible' refers to combustion.

rules.rule_name: Rekayoga Condition: 4th Lord conjunct Malefic and Combust

check_logic.condition: The planet owning the 4th bhava is in conjunction with a malefic planet and has also disappeared (combust).
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 4th house lord. Check for conjunction (within a defined orb) with any natural malefic planet AND check if the 4th lord is within the combustion orb of the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Rekayoga is not formed or its injurious effects are significantly reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 23
rules.extraction_notes: One of the conditions for the formation of Rekayoga. 'Disappear' refers to combustion.

rules.rule_name: Assess Planetary Debilitation

check_logic.condition: For every planet in the birth chart (Rashi chart).
check_logic.calculation: Identify if a planet is placed in its sign of debilitation. (e.g., Sun in Libra, Moon in Scorpio, Mars in Cancer, Mercury in Pisces, Jupiter in Capricorn, Venus in Virgo, Saturn in Aries).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of a planet's inherent strength and its ability to deliver results, leading to erroneous predictions regarding the native's life areas, especially those related to the planet's significations and house lordships. Debilitated planets generally struggle to give positive results unless there is a cancellation of debilitation (Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga).
rules.extraction_notes: The verse uses 'नीचस्थिता' (neecha-sthita) and 'नीचङ्गते' (neecha-angate) for multiple planets (Venus, Jupiter, Moon, Mars) as a key condition for specific yogas (e.g., poverty). This implies that identifying and assessing planetary debilitation is a fundamental and non-negotiable step in astrological analysis, as it significantly impacts a planet's functional capacity and the outcomes it delivers.

rules.rule_name: Combustion of 9th House Lord

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the significations of the 9th house (fortune, father, dharma, higher education) or general prosperity.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the lord of the 9th house is within the combustion orb of the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: The significations of the 9th house are severely afflicted or weakened, leading to obstacles in fortune, issues with father, or challenges in higher pursuits.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for Reka Yoga formation, highlighting a fundamental planetary state.

rules.rule_name: Debilitation of Lagna Lord

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the native's overall health, personality, vitality, and general well-being.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the lord of the rising sign (Lagna Lord) occupies its sign of debilitation.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native's health, personality, and general fortune are severely compromised, leading to inherent weaknesses or struggles in life.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for Reka Yoga formation, highlighting a fundamental planetary state for the Lagna Lord.

rules.rule_name: Debilitation of 2nd House Lord

check_logic.condition: When analyzing financial matters, family relationships, speech, or accumulated wealth.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the lord of the 2nd house occupies its sign of debilitation.

rules.impact_if_violated: The significations of the 2nd house (wealth, family, speech) are severely afflicted or weakened, leading to financial difficulties, family discord, or speech impediments.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for Reka Yoga formation, highlighting a fundamental planetary state for the 2nd Lord.

rules.rule_name: Presence of Three or More Debilitated or Combust Planets

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall strength and inherent challenges in a birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Count the number of planets that are either in their sign of debilitation or combust by the Sun. If this count is 3 or more, the condition is met.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates significant inherent weaknesses or challenges in the chart, affecting multiple areas of life and potentially leading to struggles or adverse outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for Reka Yoga formation, indicating a general affliction count.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord Placement in Dusthana (6th, 8th, or 12th House)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the native's overall well-being, health, and general fortune.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the lord of the rising sign (Lagna Lord) is placed in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native's health, personality, and general fortune are severely compromised, leading to struggles, obstacles, hidden issues, or chronic problems throughout life.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for Reka Yoga formation, explicitly mentioning 'bad position' as Dusthanas.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord Lacking Strength

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the native's inherent vitality, resilience, and ability to achieve success.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the overall strength of the Lagna Lord (e.g., through Shadbala, Avasthas, Vargabala). This rule applies if the Lagna Lord is found to be significantly weak or 'without strength' by standard astrological metrics.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native's inherent vitality, personality, and ability to overcome challenges are significantly weakened, leading to a lack of drive, poor health, or general misfortune.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for Reka Yoga formation, 'without strength' implies a general strength assessment.

rules.rule_name: Malefic Planets Afflicted by Debilitated, Inimical, or Malefic Aspects in Key Houses

check_logic.condition: When assessing the affliction to specific houses (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, 11) and their significations.
check_logic.calculation: Identify malefic planets. For each malefic planet occupying houses 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 7, 9, 10, or 11, check if it is aspected by a debilitated planet, a planet in an inimical sign, or another malefic planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The significations of the houses occupied by such afflicted malefic planets are severely damaged, leading to adverse effects related to those houses (e.g., health issues for Lagna, financial issues for 2nd, relationship issues for 7th).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for Reka Yoga formation. This rule combines planetary nature, dignity, and aspect analysis.

rules.rule_name: Identify House Lordship for all Bhavas

check_logic.condition: Before analyzing the influence of any house or its ruler.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the planetary lord for each of the 12 houses based on the ascendant sign and traditional rulerships.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of house lords will lead to fundamental errors in chart analysis and yoga formation.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is fundamental to understanding the relationships described in the verse, such as 'lord of the 8th', 'lord of the 1st', 'lord of the 9th', 'lord of the 11th'.

rules.rule_name: Assess Overall Planetary Strength

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the capacity of any planet to produce its results, good or bad, or when comparing planetary influences.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the overall strength of a planet using classical methods (e.g., Shadbala components like Sthana Bala, Kala Bala, Dig Bala, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to assess planetary strength will lead to an inaccurate understanding of a planet's ability to manifest its indications, making predictions unreliable.
rules.extraction_notes: The verse explicitly mentions comparing strengths ('exceeds... in strength') and a planet being 'weak in strength', indicating the necessity of a general strength assessment.

rules.rule_name: Check for Planetary Combustion by Sun

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the functional capacity and benefic/malefic nature of any planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is within the specified orb of the Sun, rendering it combust. The specific orb varies by planet and tradition.

rules.impact_if_violated: Ignoring combustion will lead to an overestimation of the combust planet's ability to deliver results, potentially leading to incorrect predictions, especially regarding its significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Directly mentioned as 'scorched by the Sun's rays', which is the definition of combustion.

rules.rule_name: Identify Kendra Houses (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th)

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the placement of planets and their influence on the chart's overall strength and stability.
check_logic.calculation: Identify houses 1, 4, 7, and 10 from the Lagna as Kendra houses.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misidentifying Kendra houses will lead to incorrect assessment of planetary strength, functional nature, and the overall stability of the chart.
rules.extraction_notes: The verse refers to a planet 'occupying a position other than a Kendra', necessitating the identification of Kendra houses.

rules.rule_name: Natural Planetary Nature Identification

check_logic.condition: For any astrological analysis involving planetary influences.
check_logic.calculation: Identify each planet as a natural benefic (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury when unafflicted) or a natural malefic (Saturn, Mars, Sun, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury when afflicted).

rules.impact_if_violated: Fundamental misinterpretation of the inherent auspicious or inauspicious nature of planetary influences, leading to erroneous predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata 19 (implied by repeated use of 'malefics' and 'benefics'), BPHS 3.1-5 (general planetary nature)
rules.extraction_notes: Inferred from the repeated use of 'malefics' and 'benefics' as categories in the conditions for specific yogas, indicating that this identification is a fundamental prerequisite.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity Assessment

check_logic.condition: For any planet in the birth chart or divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the planet's dignity (exaltation, debilitation, own sign, friendly sign, inimical sign, neutral sign) based on its sign placement.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of a planet's inherent strength and its ability to deliver results, leading to flawed predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata 19 (implied by 'depression or inimical signs'), BPHS 3.6-10 (general dignity rules)
rules.extraction_notes: Inferred from the explicit mention of 'depression or inimical signs' as a condition for specific yogas, indicating that dignity assessment is a fundamental prerequisite.

rules.rule_name: House Classification Assessment

check_logic.condition: For any house in the birth chart or divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: Classify houses into categories such as Kendras (1, 4, 7, 10), Trikonas (1, 5, 9), Dussthanas (6, 8, 12), Upachayas (3, 6, 10, 11), etc.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the functional significance and strength of houses, leading to incorrect analysis of life areas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata 19 (implied by 'Kendra positions' and 'Dusstthanas'), BPHS 3.15-16 (general house classification)
rules.extraction_notes: Inferred from the explicit mention of 'Kendra positions' and 'Dusstthanas (6th, 8th or 12th)' as conditions for specific yogas, indicating that house classification is a fundamental prerequisite.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Aspect Calculation

check_logic.condition: For any planet in the birth chart or divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the aspects (drishti) cast by each planet on other planets and houses based on standard Vedic astrological rules (e.g., Jupiter's 5th, 7th, 9th; Saturn's 3rd, 7th, 10th; Mars' 4th, 7th, 8th; others' 7th).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to understand the inter-connectivity and mutual influences between planets and houses, leading to incomplete or erroneous analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata 19 (explicitly mentioned 'not associated with or aspeciel by the benefics', 'not aspected by benefic planets'), BPHS 27.1-5 (general aspect rules)
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly mentioned as a condition ('not associated with or aspeciel by the benefics', 'not aspected by benefic planets'), indicating that aspect calculation is a fundamental prerequisite.

rules.rule_name: Shashtiamsha (D60) Chart Analysis

check_logic.condition: When evaluating subtle karmic influences, deep character traits, or specific yogas related to past life karma and destiny.
check_logic.calculation: Cast the Shashtiamsha (D60) chart and analyze planetary positions, dignities, and yogas within it.

rules.impact_if_violated: Missing crucial insights into an individual's deepest karmic patterns, moral inclinations, and the subtle nuances of their destiny.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly mentioned '60th portions' (Shashtiamsha) as a condition for a yoga, implying that D60 must be checked for such detailed analysis.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Debilitation Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and ability of any planet to give favorable results.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its sign of debilitation (Neecha Rashi).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's natural significations and its ability to deliver results related to its lordship and placement are severely compromised or reversed.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga is present (e.g., lord of debilitation sign is in Kendra from Lagna/Moon, or exalted, or aspected by its lord, etc.)
cancellation_conditions.impact: Debilitation effect is cancelled or reversed, leading to powerful results.

rules.extraction_notes: Extracted the concept of 'depressed in position' as a general planetary debilitation check, applied to the 12th lord in the text.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Combustion Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing the independent power and ability of any planet (except Sun and Moon) to deliver results.
check_logic.calculation: Check the angular distance between the planet and the Sun. If the planet is within specific degrees of the Sun, it is considered combust.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet loses its independent power and significations, becoming subservient to the Sun. Its ability to give good results is severely hampered or nullified.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If planet is retrograde while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced, planet may regain some power or give unexpected results.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If planet is exalted while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced, planet may still give some positive results, though its independence is still affected.

rules.extraction_notes: Extracted the concept of 'obscured by the Sun's rays' as a general planetary combustion check, applied to the 12th lord in the text.

rules.rule_name: Comparative Strength of House Lords (12th vs Lagna Lord)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the relative influence or dominance between the lord of the 12th house and the lord of the Lagna, especially in matters of financial stability, general well-being, and overall fortune.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the strength (e.g., Shadbala, Ishta/Kashta Phala, or other relevant strength metrics) of the 12th house lord and the Lagna lord. Compare their strengths.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the relative power dynamics between the houses/planets, leading to incorrect predictions regarding financial status, health, and overall fortune. A stronger 12th lord than Lagna lord can indicate losses, expenses, or difficulties dominating the native's life.
rules.extraction_notes: Directly extracted the comparison 'lord of the 12th bha va ... superior to the lord of the rising sign in strength'.

rules.rule_name: Mitigation of Malefic Yogas by Benefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: When a yoga involving malefic planets is formed.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the malefic planets forming the yoga are aspected by natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, unafflicted Mercury, waxing Moon).

rules.impact_if_violated: The malefic yoga's effects will be reduced or cancelled, leading to an incorrect assessment of the yoga's manifestation or intensity.

cancellation_conditions.condition: Malefic planets forming the yoga receive benefic aspects.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The malefic yoga's effects are reduced or cancelled.

rules.extraction_notes: This rule is explicitly stated as a condition for the manifestation of the yogas described in Sloka 49, implying a general principle for malefic yogas.

rules.rule_name: Mitigation of Malefic Yogas by Benefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: When a malefic yoga is identified in the chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planets forming the malefic yoga are aspected by natural benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, or well-placed Mercury).

rules.impact_if_violated: The severity and negative impact of the malefic yoga will be overestimated if benefic aspects are not considered, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 52
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a general principle of astrological analysis where benefic aspects can mitigate or cancel the negative effects of malefic yogas.

rules.rule_name: Planetary dignity modification: Exaltation Rashi, Debilitation Navamsa

check_logic.condition: A planet is in its exaltation sign in the Rashi (D1) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check the Navamsa (D9) position of that planet. If it is in its debilitation sign in Navamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet will produce 'depression effects' (Neecha Phala) despite being in exaltation in Rashi. Its positive results are significantly diminished or reversed.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the first part of Jataka Parijata Sloka 36.

rules.rule_name: Planetary dignity modification: Debilitation Rashi, Exaltation Navamsa

check_logic.condition: A planet is in its debilitation sign in the Rashi (D1) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check the Navamsa (D9) position of that planet. If it is in its exaltation sign in Navamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet will produce 'exaltation effects' (Uccha Phala) despite being in debilitation in Rashi. Its negative results are significantly diminished or reversed, leading to positive outcomes (Neecha Bhanga Raja Yoga).
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the second part of Jataka Parijata Sloka 36 and reinforced by Sarvarthachintamani quote.

rules.rule_name: Planetary dignity: Debilitation in Navamsa

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies its debilitation sign in the Navamsa (D9) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet's sign in Navamsa and check if it corresponds to its debilitation sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to bestow positive results related to its significations and house lordship is severely hampered. It will not bestow 'fortune' or strong positive outcomes.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the second part of the Sarvarthachintamani quote: 'उपस्थिता चेदपि नीचभागा ग्रहा न कुर्वन्ति तथैव भाग्यम्' (If planets are in debilitation Navamsa, they do not bestow such fortune). This is a general principle of planetary dignity in divisional charts.

rules.rule_name: Yogas nullified by benefic aspects

check_logic.condition: When a yoga is identified in the chart.
check_logic.calculation: Identify natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury when not conjoined with malefics). Check if any benefic planet aspects the planets forming the yoga or the house/sign where the yoga manifests.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the efficacy and outcome of a yoga, leading to erroneous predictions regarding its promise.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is a general principle stating that benefic aspects can nullify the effects of any yoga. It is crucial for accurate yoga analysis, as it modifies the inherent promise of a yoga.

rules.rule_name: Parapreshyaka Yoga Formation

check_logic.condition: When assessing the native's general disposition, independence, and potential for subservience.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the sign Makara (Capricorn) occupies the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Lagna. Additionally, check if the Moon is placed in the 4th house from the Lagna. If both conditions are met, Parapreshyaka Yoga is formed.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify this yoga will lead to an incomplete or inaccurate assessment of the native's inherent disposition, particularly regarding their autonomy and potential for being controlled by others.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 45
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the specific planetary and sign placements required for the formation of Parapreshyaka Yoga, which indicates a person who is at another's bidding.

rules.rule_name: Yoga Completion for Prediction

check_logic.condition: When predicting results based on a specific planetary combination (yoga).
check_logic.calculation: Verify all planets, houses, signs, aspects, etc., specified as components of the yoga are present as described in the classical text.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction of the yoga's full effect or manifestation.
rules.extraction_notes: General principle for yoga analysis, derived from 'The above result should not be predicted unless the characteristic combinations mentioned are complete.'

rules.rule_name: Muteness Yoga Primary Conditions

check_logic.condition: When predicting muteness based on the described yoga.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Check if all three malefics (Saturn, Mars, AND Sun) are jointly or severally in the last portion (Riksha Sandhies) of Kataka (Cancer), Vrischika (Scorpio), or Meena (Pisces). 2. Check if the Moon has a malefic aspect.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction of muteness; the yoga for muteness is not formed.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'Unless therefore all the three malefics are thus placed and the Moon has a malefic aspect, the prediction should not be made.' The definition of 'Riksha Sandhies' is incorporated into the calculation.

rules.rule_name: Muteness Yoga Cancellation by Benefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: If the primary conditions for the Muteness Yoga (Rule 2) are met.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Moon also receives aspect from one or more benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to modify the prediction from muteness to delayed speech, leading to an overly severe prediction.

cancellation_conditions.condition: Moon receives aspect from one or more benefic planets
cancellation_conditions.impact: The prediction of muteness is cancelled; the prediction changes to delayed speech.

rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'But if such Moon has the aspect of one or more benefic planets, the person will begin to speak later than when he would otherwise be naturally expected to speak.' This acts as a cancellation/modification rule for the muteness yoga.

rules.rule_name: Lunar Phase - Krishna Paksha (Dark Half)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the Moon's strength and general chart auspiciousness.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Moon is in Krishna Paksha (waning phase), which is from the Full Moon to the New Moon.

rules.impact_if_violated: Moon's natural beneficence and strength are reduced, impacting mental peace, emotional stability, and general fortune.

cancellation_conditions.condition: Moon is exalted or in own sign
cancellation_conditions.impact: Negative impact reduced

cancellation_conditions.condition: Moon is aspected by benefics
cancellation_conditions.impact: Negative impact reduced

rules.extraction_notes: Krishna Paksha Moon is generally considered weaker than Shukla Paksha Moon.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Position - Sandhi (Junction)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and efficacy of any planet.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a planet is located within a specified degree range of a sign cusp (Rashi Sandhi) or house cusp (Bhava Sandhi). Typically, 0-1 degree or 29-30 degrees of a sign, or within 1-2 degrees of a house cusp.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet loses significant strength and ability to deliver its results, often leading to confusion or lack of clarity in its significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Sandhi refers to the junction points between signs or houses, where planets are considered weak.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Uttama Varga

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's overall strength and auspiciousness.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is in Uttama Varga (best divisions). This implies a specific method of calculating varga strength, often related to Vargottama or specific auspicious divisional placements.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet's ability to give auspicious results is compromised or enhanced depending on the varga classification.
rules.extraction_notes: Uttama Varga refers to a planet being in its best divisional strength, often implying Vargottama or specific auspicious varga placements.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Airaavata Amsha (for Jupiter)

check_logic.condition: When assessing Jupiter's specific divisional strength.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if Jupiter is in Airaavata Amsha. This is a specific varga classification.

rules.impact_if_violated: Jupiter's specific auspiciousness or strength in that division is affected.
rules.extraction_notes: Airaavata Amsha is a specific divisional dignity, likely related to a particular varga chart (e.g., D10, D60) or a specific type of strength.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Placement - Kendra Houses (Angular Houses)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the general strength and influence of a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is placed in a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th). The verse mentions 'outside Kendras', implying that placement in Kendras is a fundamental check for strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planets in Kendras are generally strong and influential. Planets outside Kendras (Panapharas or Apoklimas) have reduced strength.
rules.extraction_notes: Kendras are considered pillars of the chart, and planets placed there gain strength. The verse mentions a planet *outside* Kendras, which is a specific condition, but the underlying rule is the importance of Kendra placement.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Placement - Venus in Lagna

check_logic.condition: When assessing the influence of Venus on personality and general fortune.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if Venus is placed in the 1st house (Lagna).

rules.impact_if_violated: Venus in Lagna generally bestows attractive personality, comforts, and artistic inclinations. Its absence or presence elsewhere changes these significations.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a specific house placement rule for Venus.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Conjunction - Cold Planet

check_logic.condition: When assessing the influence of a planet through association.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a planet is conjoined with a 'cold planet' (शीतयुतौ). Traditionally, Moon and Venus are considered cold/watery planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: The nature and results of the primary planet are modified by the influence of the conjoining cold planet.
rules.extraction_notes: The term 'cold planet' (शीत) typically refers to Moon or Venus due to their watery/feminine nature. This is a general rule about planetary conjunctions modifying effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Exaltation Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and impact of any planet, especially when evaluating malefic yogas or potential challenges.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the planet is in its sign of exaltation.

rules.impact_if_violated: A planet not in exaltation (or otherwise strong) may not be able to fully mitigate malefic effects or deliver its full positive potential. Exaltation provides significant inherent strength.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 59
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a cancellation condition for a malefic yoga, highlighting exaltation as a general and powerful strength factor that must be checked.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Conjunction Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and impact of any planet or house lord, especially when evaluating malefic yogas or potential challenges.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the planet is in conjunction with a natural benefic planet (e.g., Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury when with benefics).

rules.impact_if_violated: A planet not associated with benefics may struggle to mitigate malefic effects or deliver its full positive potential. Benefic conjunction provides external support and positive influence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 59
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a cancellation condition for a malefic yoga, highlighting benefic conjunction as a general and powerful strength factor that must be checked.

rules.rule_name: Combustion of 2nd Lord

check_logic.condition: When assessing financial prospects
check_logic.calculation: Check if the 2nd house lord is combust (within specific degrees of the Sun).

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates lack of wealth/poverty.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule highlights the negative impact of combustion on the house lord's significations, specifically wealth.

rules.rule_name: 2nd Lord in Dusthana (6, 8, 12) with 2nd House Affliction

check_logic.condition: When assessing financial prospects
check_logic.calculation: Check if 2nd lord is in 6th, 8th, or 12th house. Simultaneously, check if 2nd house is occupied or aspected by malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates poverty.
rules.extraction_notes: Combines planetary placement and house affliction for financial assessment.

rules.rule_name: Kendras Devoid of Benefics, Afflicted by Malefics, and 10th House Afflicted

check_logic.condition: When assessing overall fortune and financial stability
check_logic.calculation: Check if Kendras (1, 4, 7, 10) lack benefic planets and are occupied/aspected by malefics. Also check for affliction to the 10th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates poverty and general misfortune.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a complex yoga involving multiple house afflictions.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter & Moon Combust with Lagna Lord in 12th House

check_logic.condition: When assessing financial prospects and general well-being
check_logic.calculation: Check if Jupiter is combust. Check if Moon is combust. Check if Lagna lord is placed in the 12th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates poverty.
rules.extraction_notes: Combustion of key benefics (Jupiter, Moon) combined with Lagna lord in a Dusthana (12th) leads to poverty.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord & 2nd Lord in 6th, 10th Lord with Malefics, 2nd House Afflicted

check_logic.condition: When assessing financial and career prospects
check_logic.calculation: Check if Lagna lord and 2nd lord are both in the 6th house. Check if 10th lord is conjoined with malefic planets. Check if 2nd house is aspected by malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates poverty.
rules.extraction_notes: A combination of planetary placements and afflictions impacting financial and career stability.

rules.rule_name: 2nd House Afflicted by Malefics, 2nd Lord in 3rd House

check_logic.condition: When assessing financial prospects
check_logic.calculation: Check if 2nd house is occupied by malefics and aspected by malefics. Check if 2nd lord is placed in the 3rd house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates poverty.
rules.extraction_notes: Affliction to the house of wealth combined with its lord's placement in a neutral house (3rd) leading to poverty.

rules.rule_name: Malefics in all Kendras (1, 4, 7, 10) and 2nd House

check_logic.condition: When assessing overall fortune, financial stability, and progeny
check_logic.calculation: Check if all four Kendras (1, 4, 7, 10) are occupied by malefic planets. Check if the 2nd house is also occupied by a malefic planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates extreme poverty and destruction of lineage.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a severe Daridra Yoga impacting multiple aspects of life, including progeny.

rules.rule_name: Sun-Saturn-Venus Conjunction Causing Poverty

check_logic.condition: When assessing financial prospects
check_logic.calculation: Check for a conjunction of Sun, Saturn, and Venus in any house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Indicates poverty, drying up of wealth.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Sun is in Leo or Aries AND the Sun-Saturn-Venus conjunction is aspected by both Jupiter and Mercury.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The Daridra Yoga is cancelled, leading to wealth.

rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific planetary conjunction leading to poverty, with a clear cancellation condition provided in the same verse.

rules.rule_name: Sun/Moon in 12th House as Indicator for Eye Health

check_logic.condition: When assessing potential eye problems or general eye health in a chart.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the Sun or Moon (or both) are placed in the 12th bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify a specific planetary configuration indicating potential eye problems.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 56
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies a condition where Sun and Moon in the 12th house are considered to contribute to malefic effects on eyes, as part of a broader yoga for eye destruction. It's a specific indicator to be checked for health queries related to eyes.

rules.rule_name: 7th Lord in 6th with Venus Conjunction

check_logic.condition: The lord of the 7th house occupies the 6th house and is in conjunction with Venus.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the lord of the 7th house. 2. Identify the 6th house. 3. Check if the 7th lord is placed in the 6th house. 4. Check if Venus is conjoined with the 7th lord in the 6th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific marital issue (person being too weak to match wife) would not be indicated.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the first part of Sloka 76.

rules.rule_name: 6th and 1st Lords in Kendra/Trikona with Saturn Conjunction

check_logic.condition: The lords of the 6th and 1st houses are placed together in a Kendra (1, 4, 7, 10) or Trikona (1, 5, 9) house, and are conjoined with Saturn.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the lord of the 6th house. 2. Identify the lord of the 1st house. 3. Check if both 6th and 1st lords are in the same Kendra or Trikona house. 4. Check if Saturn is also conjoined with them in that same house.

rules.impact_if_violated: The indication of suffering captivity would not be made.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the second part of Sloka 76.

rules.rule_name: Sun exalted in Mesha Lagna

check_logic.condition: Sun occupies Lagna AND Lagna is Mesha (Aries)
check_logic.calculation: Check Sun's house placement and the sign of Lagna. Verify Sun's dignity (exalted in Mesha).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of native's bravery, obstinacy, and vision, as per Sun's exalted placement in Lagna.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from a specific planetary placement and dignity in Lagna.

rules.rule_name: Sun in own sign Simha Lagna

check_logic.condition: Sun occupies Lagna AND Lagna is Simha (Leo)
check_logic.calculation: Check Sun's house placement and the sign of Lagna. Verify Sun's dignity (own sign in Simha).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of native's eye health, as per Sun's placement in own sign in Lagna.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from a specific planetary placement and dignity in Lagna.

rules.rule_name: Sun debilitated in Thula Lagna

check_logic.condition: Sun occupies Lagna AND Lagna is Thula (Libra)
check_logic.calculation: Check Sun's house placement and the sign of Lagna. Verify Sun's dignity (debilitated in Thula).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of native's eye health (night-blindness) and financial status (poverty), as per Sun's debilitated placement in Lagna.

rules.rule_name: Malefic Influence on Hearing (3rd, 5th, 9th, 11th houses without benefic aspect)

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord Strength Assessment

check_logic.condition: Always assess the strength of the Lagna Lord.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the strength of the Lagna Lord using classical methods (e.g., Shadbala components). The text implies a state of 'without strength' (विबले) needs to be identified.

rules.impact_if_violated: A weak Lagna Lord indicates general debility, poor health, lack of vitality, and struggles in life, significantly impacting the overall promise of the chart and the native's well-being.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the phrase 'लग्नाधिपे च विबले' (when the lord of the Lagna is without strength), implying a necessary check for the strength/dignity of the Lagna Lord.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord's Navamsa Lordship Assessment

check_logic.condition: Always assess the Navamsa placement and its lord for the Lagna Lord.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the Lagna Lord in the Rashi chart. 2. Determine the sign occupied by the Lagna Lord in the Navamsa (D9) chart. 3. Identify the planetary lord of that Navamsa sign. 4. Determine the nature (benefic/malefic/malign) of this Navamsa lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Lagna Lord occupies a Navamsa owned by a malefic/malign planet, it indicates challenges, weaknesses, or negative influences on the Lagna Lord's significations and the native's overall destiny and character.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the phrase 'lord of the Lagna occupies a Navamsa owned by a malign planet', implying a necessary check of the D9 chart for the Lagna Lord and the nature of its Navamsa dispositor.

rules.rule_name: Vargottama Lagna/Planet Dignity Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing the intrinsic strength, dignity, or auspiciousness of a planet or the Lagna, especially when evaluating yogas that rely on such states.
check_logic.calculation: A planet or the Lagna is considered Vargottama if it occupies the same Rashi (sign) in both the Rashi chart (D1) and the Navamsa chart (D9).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to correctly identify Vargottama status leads to an inaccurate assessment of a planet's or Lagna's inherent strength and ability to deliver results, potentially misinterpreting the potential of Rajayogas or other significant combinations.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 (quoting Saravali 5, Brihatprajapatya, Brihatjataka, Jatakabharana, Bhandavya)
rules.extraction_notes: The text repeatedly uses the concept of Vargottama Lagna or Moon as a foundational condition for powerful Rajayogas. This rule defines how to validate the Vargottama state itself, which is a prerequisite for applying such yogas.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord's Strength Assessment

check_logic.condition: Lagna Lord must be powerful ('balayute').
check_logic.calculation: Assess Lagna Lord's strength using standard methods such as Shadbala, dignity (exaltation, own sign, Moolatrikona), house placement (e.g., Kendra or Kona houses), and benefic aspects. A strong Lagna Lord is fundamental for overall well-being.

rules.impact_if_violated: A weak Lagna Lord indicates a weak constitution, struggles in life, lack of general happiness, and difficulty in manifesting the chart's potential.
rules.extraction_notes: Fundamental assessment of the chart's foundation. The text states 'balayute' (powerful) without specifying a numerical threshold or a precise calculation method, implying a general strength assessment.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter's General Strength Assessment

check_logic.condition: Jupiter possesses abundant strength ('balaadye').
check_logic.calculation: Assess Jupiter's strength using standard methods such as Shadbala, dignity (exaltation, own sign, Moolatrikona), house placement (e.g., Kendra or Kona houses), and benefic aspects.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced auspiciousness, wisdom, wealth, and general well-being associated with Jupiter's significations.
rules.extraction_notes: General strength assessment for Jupiter, even if not meeting specific dignity/placement conditions. The text uses 'balaadye' (abundant strength) without specifying a numerical threshold or a precise calculation method.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter's Vargottamamsa in Lagna or 4th Bhava

check_logic.condition: Jupiter occupies Vargottamamsa AND is placed in the Lagna OR the 4th Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Jupiter's sign in the Rashi (D1) chart is the same as its sign in the Navamsa (D9) chart (Vargottamamsa). Then, verify its placement in either the Lagna (1st house) or the 4th Bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduced auspiciousness, well-being, and happiness associated with Jupiter's significations and the houses involved, compared to if this condition were met.
rules.extraction_notes: Specific dignity and placement for Jupiter contributing significantly to general well-being and happiness.

rules.rule_name: Placement of Planets in Houses of Well-being and Prosperity

check_logic.condition: Other planets (excluding Lagna Lord and Jupiter, which have specific rules) are predominantly placed in houses connected with well-being and prosperity (e.g., 2nd, 11th, and other auspicious houses).
check_logic.calculation: Assess the distribution of planets (excluding Lagna Lord and Jupiter) across benefic houses (e.g., 2nd, 11th, 5th, 9th, 10th) versus malefic houses (e.g., 6th, 8th, 12th). A higher concentration in benefic houses is favorable.

rules.impact_if_violated: General struggles or challenges in areas of life represented by the houses where planets are unfavorably placed, leading to reduced overall well-being and prosperity.
rules.extraction_notes: General principle of planetary placement for overall well-being. The text mentions '2nd, 11th and other bravas connected with well-being and prosperity' without specifying all such houses or a numerical threshold for planetary count.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity: Own Sign in Navamsa (D9)

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's inherent strength and its ability to deliver positive results, especially when evaluating yogas or general planetary efficacy.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign occupied by the planet in the Navamsa (D9) chart. If this sign is the planet's own sign (Swakshetra), the planet is considered to be in its own sign Navamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet is not in its own sign in Navamsa, its inherent strength and capacity to give full positive results are diminished. This specific dignity is a key component for certain powerful yogas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition 'occupying ... one owned by themselves' in the context of Navamsa, which contributes to a powerful yoga for kingship and wealth.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity: Not in Debilitation Sign in Navamsa (D9)

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's inherent strength and its ability to deliver positive results, especially when evaluating yogas or general planetary efficacy.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign occupied by the planet in the Navamsa (D9) chart. If this sign is the planet's sign of debilitation (Neecha Rashi), the planet is considered to be in debilitation Navamsa. For the yoga mentioned in the text, a planet must NOT be in its debilitation sign in Navamsa.

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet is in its debilitation sign in Navamsa, its inherent strength and capacity to give positive results are significantly compromised. This specific condition (not being in debilitation Navamsa) is crucial for certain powerful yogas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition 'occupying not the Navamsa of their depression sign' in the context of Navamsa, which contributes to a powerful yoga for kingship and wealth.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Exaltation Dignity

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's inherent strength and ability to give auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if a planet is placed in its sign of exaltation.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet will lack significant inherent strength and its ability to deliver auspicious results will be diminished.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a fundamental principle of planetary dignity, mentioned as a condition for strength ('strong in its exaltation').

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Dignity - No Debilitation or Enemy Sign

check_logic.condition: When assessing the deeper strength and auspiciousness of a planet, especially for D9 related matters (marriage, general fortune, dharma).
check_logic.calculation: Check the planet's sign placement in the Navamsa (D9) chart. Ensure it is not in its sign of debilitation or an enemy sign in D9.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give good results, especially for D9 significations, will be severely compromised, leading to challenges in marriage, partnerships, and general fortune.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule emphasizes the importance of D9 dignity for a planet's overall strength and auspiciousness ('Navamsa belonging neither to the depression sign nor to an enemy').

rules.rule_name: Moon's Phase (Paksha) Assessment

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the Moon's strength, beneficence, and its impact on the native's mind and general fortune.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the Moon's phase (Paksha). A full Moon (Purna Chandra) is considered strong and benefic, while a new Moon (Amavasya) is considered weak.

rules.impact_if_violated: A weak Moon (e.g., new Moon) can lead to mental instability, emotional challenges, and reduced general fortune. A strong Moon enhances these aspects.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a fundamental principle for assessing lunar strength, explicitly mentioned ('full Moon').

rules.rule_name: Planetary Placement in Kendra Houses

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's positional strength and its ability to influence the chart significantly.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a planet is placed in a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th house from Lagna).

rules.impact_if_violated: Planets not in Kendras may have reduced ability to manifest their results strongly or provide support to the chart's overall structure.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a general principle of house strength and planetary influence, implied by the condition ('strong occupying a Kendra').

rules.rule_name: Lagna Lord Strength Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall strength of the chart or specific yogas.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Lagna lord based on dignity, house placement, aspects, and Shadbala. The text implies a general assessment of 'strong'.

rules.impact_if_violated: A weak Lagna lord diminishes the overall positive potential of the chart and the strength of any yogas formed.
rules.extraction_notes: The text states 'lord of the Lagna is strong' without specifying the exact method of strength assessment, implying a general assessment of strength.

rules.rule_name: Venus in 2nd Bhava - Not Debilitated

check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign of the 2nd Bhava. Check if this sign is the sign of debilitation for Venus (Virgo).

rules.impact_if_violated: If Venus is debilitated in the 2nd Bhava, its positive effects related to wealth, family, and speech are severely hampered, and specific yogas requiring a strong Venus will not form or will be significantly weakened.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a specific condition for a 'king' yoga, but the underlying check for debilitation is a general planetary dignity rule.

rules.rule_name: Venus in 2nd Bhava - Not in Enemy Owned Sign

rules.rule_name: Moon's Navamsa Dignity - Own Sign

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Moon is not in its own Navamsa, its strength and ability to give positive results in D9 are reduced, impacting the overall promise of the chart, especially for mental peace and emotional well-being.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Navamsa Dignity - Very Friendly Sign

rules.rule_name: Moon Aspected by Venus Alone

rules.rule_name: Saturn's 11th house aspect rejection (Jataka Parijata)

check_logic.condition: When assessing Saturn's aspects on Lagna or Moon according to Jataka Parijata.
check_logic.calculation: If Saturn is in the 11th house from Lagna or Moon, its 3rd aspect on Lagna or Moon is NOT considered.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Saturn's influence on Lagna/Moon and subsequent yoga formation.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2, 2nd Adhyaya, slokas 30-31 (author's commentary)
rules.extraction_notes: This rule reflects the author's specific rejection of a commonly accepted special aspect of Saturn (3rd aspect) when it is in the 11th house from Lagna or Moon, specifically regarding its influence on Lagna/Moon.
