rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the gender of children.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from Balabhadra's addition to Verse 23, specific to progeny gender. The specific 'signs/parts' are not explicitly defined but contextually refer to progeny indicators.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Calculation Completeness

check_logic.condition: When performing or validating an Ashtakavarga calculation for any planet.
check_logic.calculation: Verify that the contributions from all eight specified sources (Lagna, Moon, Mercury, Venus, Sun, Mars, Jupiter, Saturn) have been correctly included for each sign, as they are the lords of the divisions yielding benefic dots.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Ashtakavarga chart will be fundamentally incorrect, leading to erroneous assessment of planetary and house strength, and subsequent inaccurate predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule ensures the fundamental components of Ashtakavarga are correctly accounted for as described in the text for its proper construction.

rules.rule_name: Combustion Overrides Planetary Strengths

check_logic.condition: When a planet is in an eclipsed state (combust).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is combust based on its angular distance from the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet causes sorrow or negative results, even if it possesses other strengths (e.g., benefic dots in Ashtakavarga). Its beneficial potential is nullified or severely hampered.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'अरातिनीचास्तगतो नभोगः सबिन्दुकोऽपि प्रविलापकर्ता' (specifically 'अस्तगतो' - eclipsed/combust).

rules.rule_name: Debilitation Overrides Planetary Strengths

check_logic.condition: When a planet is in its sign of debilitation.
check_logic.calculation: Check the planet's sign placement against the classical debilitation signs for that planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet causes sorrow or negative results, even if it possesses other strengths (e.g., benefic dots in Ashtakavarga). Its beneficial potential is nullified.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'अरातिनीचास्तगतो नभोगः सबिन्दुकोऽपि प्रविलापकर्ता' (specifically 'नीचास्तगतो' - debilitated).

rules.rule_name: Inimical Sign Placement Overrides Planetary Strengths

check_logic.condition: When a planet is placed in an inimical sign.
check_logic.calculation: Check the planet's sign placement against its natural enemies as per classical texts.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet causes sorrow or negative results, even if it possesses other strengths (e.g., benefic dots in Ashtakavarga). Its beneficial potential is significantly reduced.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'अरातिनीचास्तगतो नभोगः सबिन्दुकोऽपि प्रविलापकर्ता' (specifically 'अराति' - inimical).

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Sign Benefic Dots Threshold for Auspiciousness

check_logic.condition: When assessing the general auspiciousness or strength of a sign based on Ashtakavarga.
check_logic.calculation: Count the number of benefic dots (Bhinna Ashtakavarga) contributed by all planets to a specific sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: If a sign has 8 or fewer benefic dots, its general indications will be inauspicious or problematic ('व्यसनं'). If it has more than 8, it is auspicious ('शुभं').
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'अष्टाधिसंख्याधिकबिन्दवश्चेच्छुभं तद्बूने व्यसनं क्रमेण'.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Benefic Dots for Beneficial Effects

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to produce beneficial effects.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is associated with benefic dots (Bhinna Ashtakavarga) in the sign it occupies.

rules.impact_if_violated: If a planet is not associated with benefic dots, it will not produce beneficial effects ('अफलप्रदाता'). If it is associated, it produces full beneficial effects ('सर्वफलप्रदः स्यात्').
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'स्रबिन्दुगः सर्वफलप्रदः स्यादबिन्दुको यद्यफलप्रदाता'.

rules.rule_name: No Ekadhipatyasodhana if both Rasis with common lord are occupied by planets

check_logic.condition: Two Rasis (signs) that share a common lord are both occupied by at least one planet.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Rasis with common lords (e.g., Mars rules Aries & Scorpio). Check if both Rasis are occupied by any planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect application of Ekadhipatyasodhana (reduction for common lord), leading to erroneous assessment of planetary strength or house influence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 40
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from Sloka 40, first sentence: 'If the two Rasis having a common lord be both occupied by planets, no reduction need be made.'

rules.rule_name: No Ekadhipatyasodhana if one Rasi with common lord has zero benefic dots

check_logic.condition: Two Rasis (signs) that share a common lord, and one of these Rasis has zero benefic dots (e.g., in Ashtakavarga).
check_logic.calculation: Identify Rasis with common lords. Calculate benefic dots for each Rasi. Check if any of the two Rasis has 0 benefic dots.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect application of Ekadhipatyasodhana (reduction for common lord), leading to erroneous assessment of planetary strength or house influence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 40
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from Sloka 40, second sentence: 'If one of the two Rasis (whether occupied or unoccupied) have no benefic dots, then also there is no reduction.'

rules.rule_name: Ekadhipatya Reduction - One Rasi Occupied, Equal Benefic Dots

check_logic.condition: When two Rasis share a common lord (Ekadhipatya), one Rasi is occupied by a planet, the other is unoccupied, and both Rasis have the same number of benefic dots after Trikona reduction.
check_logic.calculation: The benefic dots figure in the unoccupied Rasi is removed (set to zero or disregarded).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga benefic dot calculation, leading to inaccurate planetary strength assessment (Shadbala).
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ekadhipatya reduction in Ashtakavarga, applied after Trikona reduction.

rules.rule_name: Ekadhipatya Reduction - Unoccupied Rasis with Unequal Benefic Dots

check_logic.condition: When two Rasis share a common lord (Ekadhipatya), both Rasis are unoccupied, and have an unequal number of benefic dots after Trikona reduction.
check_logic.calculation: The greater number of benefic dots in one Rasi is replaced by the lesser number of benefic dots from the other Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga benefic dot calculation, leading to inaccurate planetary strength assessment (Shadbala).
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ekadhipatya reduction in Ashtakavarga, applied after Trikona reduction.

rules.rule_name: Ekadhipatya Reduction - One Rasi Occupied with Fewer Benefic Dots

check_logic.condition: When two Rasis share a common lord (Ekadhipatya), one Rasi is occupied by a planet, and the occupied Rasi has a smaller number of benefic dots than the unoccupied Rasi after Trikona reduction.
check_logic.calculation: The number of benefic dots in the unoccupied Rasi is made equal to the number of benefic dots in the occupied Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga benefic dot calculation, leading to inaccurate planetary strength assessment (Shadbala).
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ekadhipatya reduction in Ashtakavarga, applied after Trikona reduction.

rules.rule_name: Order of Ashtakavarga Reductions: Trikona before Ekadhipatya

check_logic.condition: When performing Ashtakavarga reductions to calculate net benefic dots.
check_logic.calculation: First, perform the Trikona reduction. Second, perform the Ekadhipatya reduction on the net figures obtained after Trikona reduction.

rules.impact_if_violated: Fundamentally incorrect Ashtakavarga benefic dot calculations, leading to inaccurate planetary strength assessment (Shadbala) and erroneous predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Specifies the mandatory order of Ashtakavarga reductions.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Combustion Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing Saturn's strength and ability to give results, especially for yogas described in Jataka Parijata 32.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn is combust (in an eclipsed state).

rules.impact_if_violated: Saturn's ability to give full benefic results is significantly reduced or may give malefic results, impacting the formation or outcome of related yogas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: Refers to 'eclipsed state' which is synonymous with combustion for planets other than Rahu/Ketu.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Debilitation Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing Saturn's strength and ability to give results, especially for yogas described in Jataka Parijata 32.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn is in its sign of debilitation ('in depression').

rules.impact_if_violated: Saturn's ability to give full benefic results is significantly reduced or may give malefic results. Results may be adverse or delayed, impacting the formation or outcome of related yogas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: Refers to 'in depression' which means debilitation.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Enemy House Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing Saturn's strength and ability to give results, especially for yogas described in Jataka Parijata 32.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn is in a sign ruled by an enemy planet ('in the house of an enemy').

rules.impact_if_violated: Saturn struggles to give good results, or results are mixed/difficult, impacting the formation or outcome of related yogas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32

rules.rule_name: Saturn House Placement for Specific Yogas

check_logic.condition: When assessing the formation of specific yogas described in Jataka Parijata 32, which require Saturn to be in a particular house.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn is placed in the 1st house (Tanu Bhava) or the 5th house (Putra Bhava).

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific yogas described in the verse will not form if Saturn is not in one of these houses.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: This is a foundational placement requirement for the yogas described in the verse.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Ashtakavarga Strength Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to deliver results, especially for specific yogas or house significations, as indicated by 'benefic dots'.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the number of benefic bindus (dots) a planet receives in its Ashtakavarga chart for the house it occupies.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary strength, leading to inaccurate predictions regarding the planet's capacity to give results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: The verse uses 'benefic dots' (Bindus) as a key factor for yoga formation and results, indicating Ashtakavarga strength is a fundamental check.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Ashtakavarga Threshold for Property Yoga (4 Bindus)

check_logic.condition: When predicting acquisition of female slaves, camels, and property through the specific yoga described in Jataka Parijata 32, which requires Saturn to be in 1st/5th house, combust/debilitated/enemy house. This is one of the two required bindu counts.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn receives exactly 4 benefic bindus in its Ashtakavarga chart for the house it occupies.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific yoga for property and slaves will not form or will not give its stated results if this specific bindu count is not met (unless 5 bindus are present).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: This rule applies when Saturn is in 1st/5th house, combust, debilitated, or in an enemy house. The bindu count must be exactly 4 for this specific manifestation of the yoga.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Ashtakavarga Threshold for Property Yoga (5 Bindus)

check_logic.condition: When predicting acquisition of female slaves, camels, and property through the specific yoga described in Jataka Parijata 32, which requires Saturn to be in 1st/5th house, combust/debilitated/enemy house. This is one of the two required bindu counts.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn receives exactly 5 benefic bindus in its Ashtakavarga chart for the house it occupies.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific yoga for property and slaves will not form or will not give its stated results if this specific bindu count is not met (unless 4 bindus are present).
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: This rule applies when Saturn is in 1st/5th house, combust, debilitated, or in an enemy house. The bindu count must be exactly 5 for this specific manifestation of the yoga.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Ashtakavarga Threshold for Abundant Wealth Yoga (7 Bindus)

check_logic.condition: When predicting abundant wealth through the specific yoga described in Jataka Parijata 32, which requires Saturn to be in 1st/5th house, combust/debilitated/enemy house.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn receives exactly 7 benefic bindus in its Ashtakavarga chart for the house it occupies.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific yoga for abundant wealth will not form or will not give its stated results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: This rule applies when Saturn is in 1st/5th house, combust, debilitated, or in an enemy house. The bindu count must be exactly 7 for this specific manifestation of the yoga.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Ashtakavarga Threshold for Lordship Yoga (8 Bindus)

check_logic.condition: When predicting lordship of townships/administrative machinery through the specific yoga described in Jataka Parijata 32, which requires Saturn to be in 1st/5th house, combust/debilitated/enemy house.
check_logic.calculation: Check if Saturn receives exactly 8 benefic bindus in its Ashtakavarga chart for the house it occupies.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific yoga for lordship will not form or will not give its stated results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 32
rules.extraction_notes: This rule applies when Saturn is in 1st/5th house, combust, debilitated, or in an enemy house. The bindu count must be exactly 8 for this specific manifestation of the yoga.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Exaltation Ayus Multiplier

check_logic.condition: If a planet is in its sign of exaltation.
check_logic.calculation: Multiply the planet's Ayus (obtained by Ashtakavarga) by 2.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary strength and potential lifespan contribution, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from a general statement about Ayus calculation modification based on planetary dignity.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Debilitation Ayus Multiplier

check_logic.condition: If a planet is in its sign of debilitation.
check_logic.calculation: Multiply the planet's Ayus (obtained by Ashtakavarga) by 0.5 (halve).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary weakness and potential lifespan contribution, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from a general statement about Ayus calculation modification based on planetary dignity.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Combustion Ayus Multiplier

check_logic.condition: If a planet is combust (eclipsed by the Sun).
check_logic.calculation: Multiply the planet's Ayus (obtained by Ashtakavarga) by 0.5 (halve).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary weakness due to combustion and potential lifespan contribution, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: The term 'eclipsed' for planets other than Sun/Moon is interpreted as combustion in this context, affecting Ayus calculation.

rules.rule_name: Mars Retrogression Ayus Multiplier

check_logic.condition: If Mars is retrograde.
check_logic.calculation: Multiply Mars' Ayus (obtained by Ashtakavarga) by 2.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Mars' strength and potential lifespan contribution, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Specific rule for Mars' retrogression affecting its Ayus calculation.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Trikona Groups Definition

check_logic.condition: Before performing Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana for any planet's Ashtakavarga.
check_logic.calculation:
Identify the four fixed triangular groups of signs:
1. Mesha (Aries), Simha (Leo), Dhanus (Sagittarius)
2. Vrishabha (Taurus), Kanya (Virgo), Makara (Capricorn)
3. Mithuna (Gemini), Tula (Libra), Kumbha (Aquarius)
4. Kataka (Cancer), Vrischika (Scorpio), Meena (Pisces)

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect grouping of signs will lead to incorrect Trikona Shodhana and subsequent Ashtakavarga analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the fundamental groups required for Trikona Shodhana.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana - Equal Benefic Dots Reduction

check_logic.condition: When performing Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana for any planet's Ashtakavarga, if all three signs in a triangular group have the exact same number of benefic dots.
check_logic.calculation: If all three signs of a triangular group have the same number of benefic dots, they must all be removed (i.e., replaced by zeros).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga scores, leading to erroneous predictions regarding house/planet strength and timing of events.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is explicitly stated in Sloka 38 of Jataka Parijata and also by Parashara. It should be checked first among the reduction rules.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana - Two Zero Benefic Dots Exception (Mantraeshwara)

check_logic.condition: When performing Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana for any planet's Ashtakavarga, if two signs in a triangular group have zero benefic dots, and the 'Equal Benefic Dots Reduction' rule (check_order 1) did not apply.
check_logic.calculation: If two signs in a triangular group have zero benefic dots, the benefic dots in the third sign will remain unaltered. No reduction occurs for the third sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga scores, leading to erroneous predictions regarding house/planet strength and timing of events.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is an addition by Mantreshwara, explicitly mentioned in the text. It's a specific exception to the general reduction and should be checked after the 'Equal' rule.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana - One Zero Benefic Dot Exception

check_logic.condition: When performing Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana for any planet's Ashtakavarga, if exactly one sign in a triangular group has zero benefic dots, and the 'Equal Benefic Dots Reduction' (check_order 1) and 'Two Zero Benefic Dots Exception' (check_order 2) rules did not apply.
check_logic.calculation: If one sign in a triangular group has no benefic dots, the benefic dots in the remaining two signs of that group will keep their original figures unaltered. No reduction occurs for the other two signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga scores, leading to erroneous predictions regarding house/planet strength and timing of events.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is explicitly stated in Sloka 38 of Jataka Parijata and also by Parashara. It's an exception to the general reduction rule and should be checked after the 'Equal' and 'Two Zero' rules.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana - Least Benefic Dots Reduction

check_logic.condition: When performing Ashtakavarga Trikona Shodhana for any planet's Ashtakavarga, if none of the prior Trikona Shodhana rules (check_order 1, 2, or 3) applied, and one sign in a triangular group has the least number of benefic dots compared to the other two.
check_logic.calculation: For each triangular group, identify the sign with the least number of benefic dots. The benefic dots in the other two signs of that group must be reduced to this least number.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga scores, leading to erroneous predictions regarding house/planet strength and timing of events.
rules.extraction_notes: This is the general reduction rule, applied if no specific exception (equal, one zero, two zero) is met. Derived from Sloka 37 and 38 of Jataka Parijata, which also references Parashara's similar rule.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity (Sign-based)

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's fundamental strength and ability to deliver results.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its Moolatrikona sign, Swakshetra (own) sign, or a friendly sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: A planet not in a dignified sign (Moolatrikona, own, or friendly) will have reduced capacity to give auspicious results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 48
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from conditions for Ayus calculation, representing a general principle of planetary strength based on sign placement.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity (Varga-based)

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's strength and ability to deliver results in specific areas of life, or its overall auspiciousness.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet occupies a benefic varga (e.g., exaltation varga) or a malefic/inimical varga.

rules.impact_if_violated: A planet in a malefic or inimical varga will have its auspicious results reduced, while a planet in a benefic varga (like exaltation) will enhance its auspiciousness.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 48
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly stated for Ayus reduction if in malefic/inimical varga, and enhancement if in benefic varga (exaltation), representing a general principle of varga strength assessment.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Association with Benefics (Conjunction/Aspect)

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to deliver auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is in conjunction with or aspected by benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: A planet's ability to give auspicious results is diminished if not associated with benefics, or if associated with malefics.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 48
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the conditions for Ayus calculation, but represents a general principle of planetary strength and influence.

rules.rule_name: Ayurdaya Reduction: Planetary Association

check_logic.condition: When calculating Ayurdaya for a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet yielding Ayurdaya is associated with another planet in the same bhava (house).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation, leading to erroneous life span prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ayurdaya reduction based on planetary association in the same bhava.

rules.rule_name: Ayurdaya Reduction: Planetary Debilitation

check_logic.condition: When calculating Ayurdaya for a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet yielding Ayurdaya is debilitated (neecha).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation, leading to erroneous life span prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ayurdaya reduction based on planetary debilitation.

rules.rule_name: Ayurdaya Reduction: Planetary Combustion

check_logic.condition: When calculating Ayurdaya for a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet yielding Ayurdaya is combust (obscured by the Sun's rays).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation, leading to erroneous life span prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ayurdaya reduction based on planetary combustion.

rules.rule_name: Ayurdaya Reduction: Planet in Inimical House

check_logic.condition: When calculating Ayurdaya for a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet yielding Ayurdaya occupies an inimical house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation, leading to erroneous life span prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ayurdaya reduction based on a planet being in an inimical house. Reduction is by 1/4.

rules.rule_name: Ayurdaya Reduction: Planet in Visible Hemisphere

check_logic.condition: When calculating Ayurdaya for a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet yielding Ayurdaya is in the visible hemisphere.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation, leading to erroneous life span prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ayurdaya reduction based on a planet being in the visible hemisphere. Reduction is by 1/4.

rules.rule_name: Ayurdaya Reduction: Sun/Moon Eclipse

check_logic.condition: When calculating Ayurdaya for the Sun or the Moon.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Sun or the Moon are in conjunction with the nodes (Rahu/Ketu) and suffering an eclipse.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation for Sun or Moon, leading to erroneous life span prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for Ayurdaya reduction for Sun or Moon when afflicted by nodes during an eclipse. Reduction is by 1/4.

rules.rule_name: Ayurdaya Reduction: Greatest Reduction Principle

check_logic.condition: When multiple Ayurdaya reduction conditions are met for a single planet.
check_logic.calculation: Identify all applicable reduction percentages and apply only the highest percentage reduction, rather than cumulative reductions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation due to cumulative or incorrect application of reductions, leading to erroneous life span prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Meta-rule for applying Ayurdaya reductions: only the greatest reduction is applied if multiple conditions are met.

rules.rule_name: Bhinnashtakavarga Ayurdaya Reductions Application

check_logic.condition: When calculating Ayurdaya (longevity) from individual Bhinnashtakavargas.
check_logic.calculation: All previously mentioned reductions (as per the text) must be applied to the Ayurdaya derived from each Bhinnashtakavarga before summation.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of individual Ayurdaya components, leading to an inaccurate total longevity prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 51
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the phrase 'subject to the reductions mentioned already' in the context of Bhinnashtakavarga Ayurdaya.

rules.rule_name: Total Ayurdaya Summation from Bhinnashtakavargas

check_logic.condition: When determining the total length of life using the Bhinnashtakavarga system.
check_logic.calculation: The total length of life must be calculated by summing the net Ayurdayas obtained from each of the 7 Bhinnashtakavargas after applying all necessary reductions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect total longevity prediction due to improper summation of Bhinnashtakavarga Ayurdayas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 51
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the statement 'The total of the net Ayurdayas in the 7 Bhinnashtakavargas is the length of life'.

rules.rule_name: Mandavainashika Nakshatra Identification and Impact

check_logic.condition: When assessing potential for destruction, loss, or significant negative impact on self, relations (father, mother, siblings, friends, son, wife), or wealth.
check_logic.calculation: Perform the following calculation: (Points of the respective Karaka Bhava/Planet * a specific multiplier - previously mentioned reductions) / 27. The remainder ('tacchEshatArAshanau') and its connection to Saturn indicate a Mandavainashika Nakshatra or a critical point, signifying destruction for the relevant relation or great loss of wealth.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify inherent destructive potential for self, family members, or significant financial loss, leading to inaccurate and potentially misleading predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 51
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a complex calculation for identifying a destructive influence. The verse explicitly mentions 'Karaka Bhava Bindu', 'reductions', division by '27' (विंशत्या सह सप्तभिः), and the significance of the remainder ('tAra') and Saturn ('shanau') in causing 'destruction' (vilayam prayAnti) and 'great loss of wealth' (vipulashrInAshahEtushcha vA) for various relations.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Ashtakavarga Benefic Houses from Sun

check_logic.condition: When calculating Lagna Ashtakavarga benefic points for the Lagna Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: The 3rd, 4th, 6th, 10th, 11th, and 12th houses from the Sun's position are considered benefic places for Lagna. If Lagna falls in one of these houses, it receives a benefic point from the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ashtakavarga benefic points for Lagna, leading to flawed strength assessment and potentially inaccurate predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the list of benefic places for Lagna Ashtakavarga calculation.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavargayurdaya Application - Moon's Position

check_logic.condition: To determine the appropriate Ayurdaya (longevity) calculation method.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Moon occupies any bhava other than a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th houses) AND if the Moon is in conjunction with another planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation method will be applied, leading to erroneous longevity predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Condition for applying Ashtakavargayurdaya as the primary longevity calculation method.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavargayurdaya Application - 10th House Occupancy

check_logic.condition: To determine the appropriate Ayurdaya (longevity) calculation method.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the 10th bhava is occupied by both benefic and malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Ayurdaya calculation method will be applied, leading to erroneous longevity predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Another condition for applying Ashtakavargayurdaya as the primary longevity calculation method.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity Check

check_logic.condition: For any astrological analysis involving planetary effects.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is in its exaltation, debilitation, Moolatrikona, own, friendly, or enemy sign based on classical tables.

rules.impact_if_violated: Fundamental misinterpretation of planetary nature and capacity.
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by specific mentions of 'exaltation sign' and 'debilitated or enemy sign'.

rules.rule_name: Divisional Chart Assessment for Planetary Dignity

check_logic.condition: When assessing the detailed strength and dignity of a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate relevant divisional charts (e.g., Navamsha, Dashamsha, etc.) and assess planetary placement and dignity within them (e.g., Vargottama, Pushkaramsha, benefic Varga).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete assessment of planetary strength and capacity.
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by 'शोभनवर्गकेन्दौ' (Moon in a benefic Varga and Kendra).

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength Assessment (General)

check_logic.condition: When interpreting Dasha periods or assessing planetary capacity.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate planetary strengths (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, or other forms of strength mentioned in classical texts).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect interpretation of Dasha results and planetary capacity, especially for critical events.
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by 'सामर्थ्यहीनग्रहपाककाले' (Dasha period of a weak planet).

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Calculation Requirement

check_logic.condition: When assessing longevity, timing of death, disease, financial loss, or general transit impacts.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate Bhinnashtakavarga for all planets and Sarvashtakavarga.

rules.impact_if_violated: Fundamental error in assessing planetary strength, longevity, and timing of events.
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by repeated references to 'अष्टकवर्ग (Ashtakavarga)' and its specific applications.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Shodhana (Purification) Requirement

check_logic.condition: When using Ashtakavarga for detailed analysis, especially Pinda calculations.
check_logic.calculation: Perform Trikona Shodhana and Ekadhipatya Shodhana on Ashtakavarga.

rules.impact_if_violated: Inaccurate Ashtakavarga results and subsequent predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly stated: 'त्रिकोणैकाधिपत्यक्षशोधनं विरचय्य च' and 'पूर्वोक्तसंशोधनतो हि शुद्धं पिण्डं सुधीमान्विलिखेत्पृथक्स्वम्'.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Chidra Identification for Death Timing

check_logic.condition: When timing death.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Dasha Chidra periods (e.g., end of a Dasha, beginning of another, or specific sub-periods as defined in classical texts).

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify a critical period for death.
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly stated: 'दशाछिद्रे मृर्ति वदेत् । दशाछिद्रसमायोगे मृत्युरेव न संशयः ॥'.

rules.rule_name: Saturn Ashtakavarga Zero Benefic Dots Check for Transit Impact

check_logic.condition: When analyzing Saturn's transits for longevity, financial loss, or general adverse events.
check_logic.calculation: Identify signs in Saturn's Bhinnashtakavarga that have 0 benefic dots.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of death or major financial loss during Saturn's transit.
rules.extraction_notes: Stated: 'अष्टकवर्ग (Ashtakavarga) of Saturn, there be a sign thoroughly void of benefic dots, the person born will meet with death or sustain loss of wealth when the planet passes through the sign in question'.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Sarvashtakavarga Dots and Strength for Birth Sorrow/Wealth Loss

check_logic.condition: When assessing general well-being, wealth, or happiness from birth.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate Sarvashtakavarga for Lagna. Assess Lagna strength (e.g., Shadbala, Varga strength).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of birth conditions and early life challenges.
rules.extraction_notes: Stated: 'षट्पञ्चविन्दुसहिते तनुगे बलात्ये जन्मादिदुःखविपुलं धननाशमेति' (If Lagna has 5 or 6 benefic dots and is strong, there will be much sorrow from birth and loss of wealth).

rules.rule_name: Short-Lived Child Condition (Kendra Exaltation, Low Ashtakavarga Dots)

check_logic.condition: When assessing child longevity.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets in Kendras. Check if the Kendra is the planet's exaltation sign. Check the planet's Bhinnashtakavarga dots in that sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of child's longevity.
rules.extraction_notes: Stated: 'if the planet occupying a Kendra be associated with one, two, three or four benefic dots and if the Kendra be the planet's exaltation sign, any child delivered from the womb at such a time will be short-lived'.

rules.rule_name: Sarvashtakavarga Zero Dots Check for Transit Impact

check_logic.condition: When analyzing transits for general adverse results.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate Sarvashtakavarga. Identify houses with 0 benefic dots.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of transit results.
rules.extraction_notes: Stated: 'समूलाष्टकवर्गे च यत्र नास्ति फलं गृहे । तत्र नास्ति फलं तस्य यदा याति शनैश्वरः ॥' (In the Sarvashtakavarga, if there is no benefic dot in a house, there will be no good result for the person when Saturn transits that house).

rules.rule_name: Ayur Pinda Calculation for Longevity

check_logic.condition: When performing detailed longevity assessment.
check_logic.calculation: Perform Shodhya Pinda calculation. Multiply by benefic dots in the 8th house (from Lagna in Sarvashtakavarga). Divide by 27. If the result is above 100, subtract 100. This result is Ayur Pinda.

rules.impact_if_violated: Gross error in longevity calculation.
rules.extraction_notes: Detailed calculation steps provided in the text: 'शोध्यादिगुणनं कृत्वा पिण्डं संस्थाप्य यत्नतः ॥ अष्टमस्थफलैहत्वा सप्तविंशतिभाजितम् । शतादृर्ध्व तु तत्पिण्डं शतमेव त्यजेदतः ॥ आयुःपिण्डं तु जानीयात्'.

rules.rule_name: Pinda Calculation for Death Timing

check_logic.condition: When timing death using Pinda methods.
check_logic.calculation: Take a Pinda (e.g., Ayur Pinda). Multiply by benefic dots in the 8th house from Lagna (in Sarvashtakavarga). Divide by 27. The remainder indicates the time of death.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of death.
rules.extraction_notes: Stated: 'पिण्डं संस्थाप्य गुणयेल्लग्नादष्टमगैः फलैः । सप्तविंशतिहृच्छेषं मृत्युकालं वदेहुधः ॥'.

rules.rule_name: Sarvashtakavarga Bhava Strength - Void Effect

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential results of a specific bhava based on Sarvashtakavarga.
check_logic.calculation: Count the total number of benefic dots (bindus) in the bhava in the Sarvashtakavarga chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrectly predicting positive or even medium results for a bhava that is actually void of effect, leading to false hopes or misguidance.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 53
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Sarvashtakavarga effects: 'up to 25 appearing in any bhava are void of effect'.

rules.rule_name: Sarvashtakavarga Bhava Strength - Medium Effect

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential results of a specific bhava based on Sarvashtakavarga, and the benefic dots are greater than 25.
check_logic.calculation: Count the total number of benefic dots (bindus) in the bhava in the Sarvashtakavarga chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misjudging the potential of a bhava as either very strong or very weak, leading to inaccurate predictions about moderate outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 53
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Sarvashtakavarga effects: 'From 25 to 30 benefic dots in a bhava produce medium effect'.

rules.rule_name: Sarvashtakavarga Bhava Strength - Strong Effect

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential results of a specific bhava based on Sarvashtakavarga.
check_logic.calculation: Count the total number of benefic dots (bindus) in the bhava in the Sarvashtakavarga chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Underestimating the positive potential of a bhava, leading to missed opportunities or failure to advise on significant gains in happiness, wealth, or fame.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 53
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Sarvashtakavarga effects: 'More than 30 benefic dots in any bhava produce happiness, wealth and fame'.

rules.rule_name: Sign Classification for Auspicious Actions

check_logic.condition: When selecting an auspicious sign (Rashi) for initiating any significant action or event.
check_logic.calculation: Signs are classified as 'best' (श्रेष्ठा) if they possess more than 30 points/aksas, 'worst' (कष्टा) if they possess less than 25 points/aksas (शरकृति), and 'medium' (मध्या) otherwise. The specific system for these points/aksas is not detailed in this verse. The 'best' signs should be chosen, and 'worst' signs should be avoided for actions like travel.

rules.impact_if_violated: Initiating actions in 'worst' signs can lead to adverse outcomes or difficulties for the endeavor.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 54
rules.extraction_notes: The exact numerical criteria for 'अधिक' (more than) 30 and 'ऊन' (less than) 25 (शरकृति) for classifying signs, and the specific type of 'aksas' or 'points' referred to, are not fully specified in this verse. However, the principle of classification and its application in electional astrology is clear.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength Override by Low Bindus

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall strength and beneficence of a planet, even if it is otherwise well-placed (e.g., in exaltation, own sign, friend's sign, or in Kendra, Trikona, or Upachaya houses, and possessing strength from auspicious vargas).
check_logic.calculation: A planet, despite having good dignity and auspicious house placement, will yield destructive (नाशदा) results if it possesses low bindus (लाघवबिन्दुकाश्चेत्). The bindus (e.g., Ashtakavarga bindus) must be assessed to confirm the planet's actual capacity to deliver benefic results.

rules.impact_if_violated: A planet that appears strong due to dignity and placement will fail to deliver benefic results, or may even cause destruction, if its bindu strength is low. This negates otherwise positive indications.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 54
rules.extraction_notes: The specific type of 'bindus' (e.g., Ashtakavarga) and the numerical threshold for what constitutes 'low' bindus are not specified in this verse. However, the principle that bindu strength can override other forms of dignity and placement is clearly stated.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Dusthana or 7th house is malefic

check_logic.condition: A planet occupying the 6th, 8th, 12th, or 7th house from the Lagna is considered malefic.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the house occupied by the planet from the Lagna. Check if it is 6th, 7th, 8th, or 12th.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's significations and house lordships will suffer or cause adverse results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 55
rules.extraction_notes: The text explicitly includes the 7th house as a malefic position, alongside 6th, 8th, 12th.

rules.rule_name: Planet in varga of its depression sign is malefic

check_logic.condition: A planet occupying a varga (divisional chart) where it is debilitated is considered malefic.
check_logic.calculation: For each planet, determine its sign placement in relevant divisional charts (e.g., D1, D9, D10, etc.). Check if this sign is its sign of debilitation.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give good results in the specific area represented by the divisional chart is severely compromised.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 55
rules.extraction_notes: Refers to 'vargas of their depression signs'. This implies checking dignity in divisional charts.

rules.rule_name: Planet in varga of a malefic planet is malefic

check_logic.condition: A planet occupying a varga (divisional chart) that belongs to a natural malefic planet is considered malefic.
check_logic.calculation: For each planet, identify the lord of the sign it occupies in a divisional chart. Determine if this lord is a natural malefic (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Sun, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury with malefics).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's results in the specific area of the divisional chart will be tainted by malefic influence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 55
rules.extraction_notes: Refers to 'vargas of malefic planets'. This implies checking the lord of the sign occupied by the planet in a divisional chart.

rules.rule_name: Planet in varga of an inimical planet is malefic

check_logic.condition: A planet occupying a varga (divisional chart) that belongs to a planet inimical to it is considered malefic.
check_logic.calculation: For each planet, identify the lord of the sign it occupies in a divisional chart. Determine if this lord is an enemy to the planet in question (based on natural planetary relationships).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's results in the specific area of the divisional chart will be hindered by inimical influence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 55
rules.extraction_notes: Refers to 'vargas of inimical planets'. This implies checking the lord of the sign occupied by the planet in a divisional chart and its relationship to the planet.

rules.rule_name: Planet in conjunction with the lord of Mandi's sign is malefic

check_logic.condition: A planet in conjunction with the lord of the sign where Mandi is placed is considered malefic.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Calculate Mandi's position. 2. Identify the lord of the sign Mandi occupies. 3. Check if any planet is in conjunction with this lord (typically within a certain orb, e.g., 5-10 degrees).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's significations will be severely afflicted, leading to adverse outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 55
rules.extraction_notes: Based on 'ये मान्दिराशिपतिना सह बाधकाच' from the Sanskrit, which translates to 'those that conspire in conjunction with the lord of the sign occupied by Mandi'.

rules.rule_name: Sarvashtakavarga benefic dots for house strength

check_logic.condition: A house with insufficient benefic dots in Sarvashtakavarga can cause a planet placed there to do evil, even if otherwise well-placed.
check_logic.calculation: Sum the benefic dots (bindus) for each house in Sarvashtakavarga. A house is generally considered strong if it has 28 or more benefic dots. Check if the total benefic dots for a house are below this threshold.

rules.impact_if_violated: Even if otherwise well-placed, a planet in a house with insufficient benefic dots will struggle to deliver positive results or may cause adverse effects.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 55
rules.extraction_notes: The text states a planet 'may do evil if associated with insufficient benefic dots (in the सर्वाष्टकवर्ग-Sarvashtakavarga)'. This implies a validation check for the number of benefic dots, with 28 being the common threshold for sufficiency.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga Benefic Dot Requirement for Beneficial Planetary Results

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's capacity to deliver beneficial results, its Ashtakavarga strength must be considered.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the number of benefic dots (bindus) a planet receives in its Ashtakavarga chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misjudgment of a planet's inherent strength and its ability to produce positive outcomes, leading to inaccurate predictions regarding beneficial results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Verse 56
rules.extraction_notes: The rule states that planets associated with a 'higher number of benefic dots' produce beneficial results. This implies that the number of benefic dots is a critical factor in determining a planet's ability to give positive outcomes. The exact threshold for 'higher' is not specified in this verse, making it a relative assessment rather than an absolute numerical threshold. The 'three classes of planets' mentioned are not defined in this specific verse, but the principle of Ashtakavarga strength is generally applicable.

rules.rule_name: Sun's Prastharashtaka Varga Bindu Contribution

check_logic.condition: When Sun is in Mesha sign
check_logic.calculation: Sun contributes 1 bindu to Sun's Prastharashtaka Varga.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect calculation of Sun's Prastharashtaka Varga, leading to an inaccurate assessment of Sun's strength and beneficence in a specific sign.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from Sun's Prastharashtaka Varga table. Each '1' indicates a bindu contribution from the row planet/Lagna when the Sun is in the column sign.

rules.rule_name: Sarvashtaka Varga (SAV) benefic dots threshold for specific yogas (Kingship)

check_logic.condition: When evaluating specific yogas involving house lords and their placement for high status or kingship, particularly the yoga described where the lord of the 4th is in Lagna and Lagna lord is in the 4th.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the Sarvashtaka Varga (SAV) benefic dots for the Rasis occupied by the Lagna and 4th house lords involved in the yoga.

rules.impact_if_violated: The specific yoga's promise of high status or kingship may not fully manifest or be significantly weaker if the SAV benefic dots do not meet this threshold.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 62
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a specific yoga description, focusing on the SAV threshold as a validation point for the yoga's strength and manifestation.

rules.rule_name: Sarvashtaka Varga (SAV) benefic dots threshold for power and prosperity (Houses 1, 4, 11)

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the potential for abundant power, prosperity, and high status (culminating in kingship), especially for manifestation after the 10th year.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the Sarvashtaka Varga (SAV) benefic dots for the 1st, 4th, and 11th bhavas.

rules.impact_if_violated: The promise of abundant power, prosperity, and kingship may not manifest or be significantly weaker if the SAV benefic dots in the 1st, 4th, and 11th bhavas do not exceed this threshold.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 63
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a specific yoga description, focusing on the SAV threshold for specific houses as a validation point for the yoga's strength and manifestation.

rules.rule_name: Lagna Benefic Aspect Assessment for Life Span Adjustment

check_logic.condition: When calculating or assessing the duration of life (life span) for an individual.
check_logic.calculation: Examine the aspects of benefic planets upon the Lagna (Ascendant). The presence and strength of such aspects are used to adjust the calculated life span (by diminution or addition of a multiple of 27 years).

rules.impact_if_violated: The life span calculation will be inaccurate or incomplete, leading to an incorrect assessment of the individual's longevity.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 72
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies a mandatory adjustment step for life span calculations, emphasizing the critical role of benefic aspects on the Lagna for accurate longevity assessment.

rules.rule_name: Nakshatra Ayus Longevity Adjustment

check_logic.condition: WHEN calculating Nakshatra Ayus (life span) according to the prescribed method, and the initial calculated value exceeds the standard Ayus of 100 years.
check_logic.calculation: Subtract 100 years from the initial Nakshatra Ayus value.

rules.impact_if_violated: The final Nakshatra Ayus value will be incorrect, leading to an erroneous longevity prediction.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 2 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 71
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies a mandatory adjustment to the calculated Nakshatra Ayus if it exceeds 100 years, ensuring the final value adheres to the prescribed standard. This is a conditional step within the longevity calculation procedure.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity Check - Debilitation

check_logic.condition: For every planet in the birth chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its sign of debilitation. (e.g., Sun in Libra, Moon in Scorpio, Mars in Cancer, Mercury in Pisces, Jupiter in Capricorn, Venus in Virgo, Saturn in Aries).

rules.impact_if_violated: A debilitated planet is significantly weakened, struggles to deliver positive results related to its significations and house lordships, and may cause difficulties or adverse outcomes unless cancellation conditions (Neecha Bhanga) are present.
rules.extraction_notes: The text mentions 'अष्टमाधिपतिर्नीचे' (8th lord debilitated) as a condition for death prediction. This implies the fundamental importance of checking a planet's debilitation status as a general validation rule for assessing planetary strength and promise, even though the text applies it in a specific predictive context and does not provide a full table of debilitation signs.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Auspiciousness by Benefic Influence

check_logic.condition: To determine if a Bhava (house) will yield auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: A Bhava is considered auspicious if it is occupied or aspected by natural benefics (Venus, Mercury, Jupiter) AND it is NOT occupied or aspected by any other planets (implying natural malefics).

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Bhava is not exclusively influenced by natural benefics, or is influenced by malefics, its results will not be purely auspicious and may be mixed or inauspicious.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the primary condition for a Bhava to give auspicious results based on the exclusive influence of natural benefics (Venus, Mercury, Jupiter) and the absence of malefic influence.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Destruction by Debilitated or Inimical Planet

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and potential of a bhava (house) based on its lord's dignity.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet that owns a specific bhava. Check if this planet is placed in its sign of debilitation (Neecha Rashi) or an inimical sign (Ripu Rashi).

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava owned by the planet will be destroyed or suffer significantly, indicating challenges or lack of fulfillment related to that house's significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the first part of the verse regarding planets in depression or inimical signs.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Promotion by Exalted, Moolatrikona, Own, or Friendly Planet

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and potential of a bhava (house) based on its lord's dignity.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the planet that owns a specific bhava. Check if this planet is placed in its Moolatrikona sign, own sign (Swa Rashi), exalted sign (Uccha Rashi), or a friendly sign (Mitra Rashi).

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava owned by the planet will be promoted or enhanced, indicating strength, positive outcomes, and fulfillment related to that house's significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the second part of the verse regarding planets in Moolatrikona, own, exalted, or friendly signs.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Flourishing Condition Assessment

check_logic.condition: To assess if a Bhava (house) is in a flourishing condition, indicating strong positive potential for its significations.
check_logic.calculation:
For a Bhava to be in a flourishing condition, its lord must satisfy ALL of the following criteria:
1.  **Positional Strength**: The Bhava lord must be located in a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th house) OR a Trikona (1st, 5th, 9th house) from the Lagna.
2.  **Dignity/Aspectual Strength**: The Bhava lord must be either aspected by benefic planets OR be in its exaltation sign or other strong Vargas (e.g., own sign, Moolatrikona, friendly sign, Vargottama).
3.  **Overall Strength**: The Bhava lord must possess abundant strength (baladhyah), which is generally assessed through Shadbala and other strength considerations.

rules.impact_if_violated: If these conditions are not met, the Bhava will not be considered in a flourishing condition, indicating potential weakness, challenges, or reduced positive outcomes related to its significations.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule combines positional strength, aspectual strength, dignity, and overall strength of a Bhava lord to determine the Bhava's condition. 'Abundant strength' is a general term that implies a planet is strong in Shadbala and other forms of strength.

rules.rule_name: Mitigation of Adverse Navamsa Placement for Planetary Beneficence

check_logic.condition: A planet is placed in a Navamsa sign that is owned by a malefic planet, inimical to the planet, or is the planet's sign of debilitation.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the Navamsa sign of the planet. 2. Determine if the Navamsa sign's lord is a malefic, inimical to the planet, or if the sign itself is the planet's debilitation sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the planet is in an adverse Navamsa placement AND none of the cancellation conditions are met, the planet will likely prove adverse or weak in delivering its significations, especially concerning marriage, partnerships, and general fortune (Navamsa significations).

cancellation_conditions.condition: The Navamsa lord (Navatisa) is in its own sign, exaltation sign, or a friendly planet's sign.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The planet will nevertheless prove beneficial despite its difficult Navamsa placement.

cancellation_conditions.condition: The Rasi occupied by the planet is its own sign, exaltation sign, or a friendly planet's sign.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The planet will nevertheless prove beneficial despite its difficult Navamsa placement.

cancellation_conditions.condition: Benefic planets aspect the planet.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The planet will nevertheless prove beneficial despite its difficult Navamsa placement.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 10
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies conditions under which a planet, despite being in an adverse Navamsa placement (malefic, inimical, or debilitated sign), can still yield beneficial results. The cancellation conditions are independent and any one of them is sufficient to override the adverse effect.

rules.rule_name: Planets in a house cannot promote its affairs if house lord is weak/afflicted

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the potential and outcomes of a specific Bhava (house) and the influence of planets placed within it.
check_logic.calculation:
Identify the planetary lord of the Bhava in question. Check if this Bhava lord is afflicted by any of the following conditions: 
1.  **Placement in a Dusthana**: The Bhava lord is situated in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Lagna or from its own sign. 
2.  **Placement in an Inimical Sign**: The Bhava lord is placed in a sign ruled by a planet inimical to it. 
3.  **Combustion**: The Bhava lord is combust (within specific degrees of the Sun). 
4.  **General Weakness**: The Bhava lord is generally weak (e.g., debilitated, low Shadbala, in a bad avastha, or otherwise lacking strength).

rules.impact_if_violated: Planets placed in the Bhava will be unable to significantly advance or promote the affairs of that Bhava, even if they are otherwise well-placed or strong. The Bhava's potential for positive outcomes will be severely diminished.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 8
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the conditions under which planets in a house cannot promote its affairs, specifically when the house lord is afflicted. 'मूढे' is interpreted as combust, and 'दुर्बले' as general weakness, which can include debilitation.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Prosperity by Lord's Aspect

check_logic.condition: To assess the potential for positive outcomes related to a specific Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava is aspected by its own lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava's potential for positive results is diminished or absent if this condition is not met.
rules.classical_reference: Uttara Parashara (quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 3)
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Sanskrit quote 'यो यो भावः स्वामिदृष्टो...' attributed to Uttara Parashara.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Prosperity by Lord's Conjunction

check_logic.condition: To assess the potential for positive outcomes related to a specific Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava is conjoined with its own lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava's potential for positive results is diminished or absent if this condition is not met.
rules.classical_reference: Uttara Parashara (quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 3)
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Sanskrit quote '...युतो वा...' attributed to Uttara Parashara.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Prosperity by Benefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: To assess the potential for positive outcomes related to a specific Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava is aspected by benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava's potential for positive results is diminished or absent if this condition is not met.
rules.classical_reference: Uttara Parashara (quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 3)
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Sanskrit quote '...सौम्यैर्वा स्यात्तस्य तस्याभिवृद्धिः' attributed to Uttara Parashara.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Prosperity by Benefic Conjunction

check_logic.condition: To assess the potential for positive outcomes related to a specific Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava is conjoined with benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava's potential for positive results is diminished or absent if this condition is not met.
rules.classical_reference: Uttara Parashara (quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 3)
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Sanskrit quote '...सौम्यैर्वा स्यात्तस्य तस्याभिवृद्धिः' attributed to Uttara Parashara.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Loss by Malefic Aspect

check_logic.condition: To assess the potential for negative outcomes or challenges related to a specific Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava is aspected by malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava's potential for positive results is significantly diminished, and negative results are likely.
rules.classical_reference: Uttara Parashara (quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 3)
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Sanskrit quote 'पॉवेरेवं तस्य भावस्य हानिर्निर्देष्टव्या...' and 'न पापखेटेक्षितसंयुतेषु' attributed to Uttara Parashara.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Loss by Malefic Conjunction

check_logic.condition: To assess the potential for negative outcomes or challenges related to a specific Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava is conjoined with malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava's potential for positive results is significantly diminished, and negative results are likely.
rules.classical_reference: Uttara Parashara (quoted in Jataka Parijata Vol 3)
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the Sanskrit quote 'पॉवेरेवं तस्य भावस्य हानिर्निर्देष्टव्या...' and 'न पापखेटेक्षितसंयुतेषु' attributed to Uttara Parashara.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Lord in Dignified State for Bhava Strength

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and promise of a bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the lord of the bhava is placed in its Moolatrikona sign, exaltation sign, or a friendly sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava's promise and positive manifestations will be weaker than optimal, or require more effort.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'मूलत्रिकोण (Moolatrikona), exaltation or friendly sign it advances the same.'

rules.rule_name: Bhava Lord in Debilitated or Weak State for Bhava Weakness

check_logic.condition: When assessing the inherent strength and promise of a bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the lord of the bhava is placed in an enemy sign, its sign of debilitation, or is combust (मूढो).

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava's promise will be significantly weakened, leading to struggles, delays, or negative outcomes related to that bhava.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'यद्भावेशोऽरिनीचस्थो मूढो वा तन्न पश्यति ।'

rules.rule_name: Bhava Strength from Lord & Karaka Dignity and Benefic Influence

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall strength and positive potential of a bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava lord AND its Karaka (significator) are simultaneously in their own house, exaltation sign, or mutually placed, AND are conjoined or aspected by benefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava's promise will be weaker than optimal, lacking robust support and positive outcomes.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'भावेशः कारकोऽपि स्वगृहमुपगतौ तुङ्गमङ्गौ यदा ता- वन्योन्यस्थौ शुभाभ्यामभित इतियुतौ वीक्षिनौ सौम्यदृष्टौ । यद्येवं भावपुष्टिर्भवति'

rules.rule_name: Bhava Weakness from Lord & Karaka Debility and Malefic Influence

check_logic.condition: When assessing the overall strength and potential challenges of a bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Bhava lord AND its Karaka are simultaneously weak, placed in Dusthanas (6th, 8th, 12th houses), conjoined or aspected by malefic planets, or in enemy/debilitation/combustion.

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava will suffer significant loss, detriment, or severe challenges related to its significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'यदि हिनौ दुःस्थत्रस्थौ युतौ वा दृष्टौ पापापचारैर्न भवति रिपुनीचाम्तयुक्तौ च हानिः ॥'

rules.rule_name: Bhava Annihilation due to Lord in Dusthana

check_logic.condition: When assessing the promise and potential of any bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the lord of the bhava is placed in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava will suffer annihilation or severe detriment, leading to significant challenges or lack of positive results related to its significations.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If the bhava (or its lord in dusthana) is aspected by a benefic planet.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The annihilation effect is mitigated, reducing the severity of negative outcomes.

rules.extraction_notes: Directly from English translation of Sloka 4 and Sl. 5-6 for cancellation.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Annihilation due to Dusthana Lord in Bhava

check_logic.condition: When assessing the promise and potential of any bhava.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the bhava is occupied by the lord of the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: The bhava will suffer annihilation or severe detriment, leading to significant challenges or lack of positive results related to its significations.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If the bhava is aspected by a benefic planet.
cancellation_conditions.impact: The annihilation effect is mitigated, reducing the severity of negative outcomes.

rules.extraction_notes: Directly from English translation of Sloka 4 and Sl. 5-6 for cancellation.

rules.rule_name: Planet Combustion Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to deliver results or advance bhava strength.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is within the combustion degrees of the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet's strength and ability to deliver auspicious results are severely compromised or negated. Its ability to advance bhava strength is severely compromised.
rules.extraction_notes: The term 'eclipsed' is interpreted as combustion.

rules.rule_name: Planet Dignity - Unfriendly Sign Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to deliver results or advance bhava strength.
check_logic.calculation: Check the planet's sign placement against its natural friendships/enmities.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet's strength and ability to deliver auspicious results are compromised. Its ability to advance bhava strength is compromised.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule implies checking planetary dignity based on sign lordship and natural relationships, which are typically found in table-based data.

rules.rule_name: Planet Dignity - Debilitation Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to deliver results or advance bhava strength.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is placed in its sign of debilitation.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet's strength and ability to deliver auspicious results are severely compromised or negated. Its ability to advance bhava strength is severely compromised.
rules.extraction_notes: The term 'in depression' is interpreted as debilitation. This rule implies checking planetary dignity based on specific debilitation signs, which are typically found in table-based data.

rules.rule_name: Bhava Effect Fullness Check

check_logic.condition: When assessing if a planet produces the *full* effect of the bhava it occupies.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the absolute difference between the planet's distance from the bhava-sandhi and the bhavamsa (semibhava).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet will not produce the full effect of the bhava; its effect must be proportionally adjusted (reduced) using a rule-of-three process.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the condition for a planet to produce the *full* effect of a bhava. If the calculated difference is greater than the threshold (0), the condition for full effect is violated, and the effect is partial.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity and Benefic Vargas for Remarkable Positive Outcomes

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for remarkable positive outcomes related to a planet's significations (e.g., remarkable celebrity, wealth, success).
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet has attained its exaltation and occupies benefic vargas (benefic divisional strengths).

rules.impact_if_violated: The native will not achieve remarkable positive outcomes, or the positive outcome will be significantly diminished.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 18
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for 'remarkable celebrity' which requires exaltation and benefic vargas.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength for Positive Outcomes of Significators

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for a positive outcome related to a house lord or Karaka (e.g., a renowned brother, successful father). The planet(s) involved must possess strength.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the overall strength of the relevant Karaka (e.g., Mars for brother) and the lord of the concerned house (e.g., 3rd house lord for brother). This implies a general strength assessment (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas).

rules.impact_if_violated: The positive outcome (e.g., a renowned brother) may not materialize, or its extent will be diminished.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 19
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for a 'renowned brother' which explicitly states 'possess strength' for the Karaka and house lord.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Placement and Conjunction for Ease and Positive Outcomes

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for ease, comfort, or general positive outcomes related to a planet's significations, especially concerning travel.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet occupies a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) or Trikona (1st, 5th, 9th) house AND is in conjunction with a benefic planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native may not experience ease in the related matters (e.g., travel), or the positive outcome will be absent or difficult to achieve.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 18
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the condition for 'ease in travelling' which requires placement in Kendra/Trikona and conjunction with a benefic.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Combustion Assessment

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the strength and influence of any planet.
check_logic.calculation: A planet is considered combust when it is within a specific degree range of the Sun, causing it to lose its ability to give full results. The exact degree range varies by planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify combustion will lead to an overestimation of the planet's strength and an incorrect prediction of its significations and house lordships.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 24
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the phrase 'eclipsed by the Sun'.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength Assessment (General)

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the capacity of any planet to deliver its results.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the planet's overall strength using classical methods such as Shadbala (Positional, Directional, Temporal, Natural, Aspectual, Exaltation strength) or other relevant strength metrics.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of a planet's strength will lead to erroneous predictions regarding its ability to manifest its significations and house lordships.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 24
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the phrase 'possessed of strength'.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity Assessment (Exaltation, Own, Friend's House)

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the inherent quality and ease with which a planet operates.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if a planet is placed in its sign of exaltation, its own sign (Moolatrikona or Swakshetra), or a friendly sign. This contributes significantly to its inherent dignity and functional strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misjudging a planet's dignity will lead to incorrect interpretations of its natural tendencies and its capacity to give auspicious or inauspicious results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 24
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the phrase 'in a Rasi which is a friend's house, its own or its exaltation sign'.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Enmity Check for Foreign Residence

check_logic.condition: To assess the promise of foreign residence, specifically if the native goes to a foreign country.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the Lagna Lord. 2. Identify the house occupied by the Lagna Lord. 3. Identify the 12th house from the house identified in step 2. 4. Identify the lord of the house identified in step 3 (Relevant 12th Lord). 5. Check if this Relevant 12th Lord is an enemy to the Lagna Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the promise of foreign residence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 23
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a predictive statement, identifying the underlying condition as a validation rule.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Debilitation Check for Foreign Residence

check_logic.condition: To assess the promise of foreign residence, specifically if the native goes to a foreign country.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the Lagna Lord. 2. Identify the house occupied by the Lagna Lord. 3. Identify the 12th house from the house identified in step 2. 4. Identify the lord of the house identified in step 3 (Relevant 12th Lord). 5. Check if this Relevant 12th Lord is in depression (debilitated).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the promise of foreign residence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 23
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a predictive statement, identifying the underlying condition as a validation rule.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Weakness Check for Foreign Residence

check_logic.condition: To assess the promise of foreign residence, specifically if the native goes to a foreign country.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the Lagna Lord. 2. Identify the house occupied by the Lagna Lord. 3. Identify the 12th house from the house identified in step 2. 4. Identify the lord of the house identified in step 3 (Relevant 12th Lord). 5. Check if this Relevant 12th Lord is weak (e.g., low Shadbala, in inimical sign, combust, etc., based on general astrological principles of planetary strength).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the promise of foreign residence.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 23
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a predictive statement, identifying the underlying condition as a validation rule. 'Weak' is a general term, implying a comprehensive strength assessment.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Duhsthana

check_logic.condition: Check if a planet is placed in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the house placement of the planet relative to the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduces the benefic results of the planet and the house it rules/aspects.
rules.extraction_notes: Duhsthana (6th, 8th or 12th place from it) - 'from it' usually implies from Lagna unless specified otherwise.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Inimical Sign

check_logic.condition: Check if a planet is placed in a sign that is inimical to it.
check_logic.calculation: Refer to planetary friendship/enmity tables to determine if the planet's sign placement is inimical.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduces the benefic results and strength of the planet.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'inimical house' condition.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Eclipse (Combustion or Rahu/Ketu Conjunction)

check_logic.condition: Check if a planet is in a state of eclipse (combust due to proximity to Sun or closely conjunct Rahu/Ketu).
check_logic.calculation: Calculate angular distance between the planet and Sun for combustion, or between the planet and Rahu/Ketu for conjunction.

rules.impact_if_violated: Significantly weakens the planet and its significations, potentially causing adverse results.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'in a state of eclipse' condition.

rules.rule_name: Planet Associated with Malefics

check_logic.condition: Check if a planet is associated with natural malefics (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Sun, Rahu, Ketu, afflicted Mercury, waning Moon) through conjunction or close aspect.
check_logic.calculation: Determine conjunctions or close aspects with identified malefic planets.

rules.impact_if_violated: Significantly reduces the benefic results of the planet and can cause affliction to its significations.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'पाप' (malefic) in 'पापारिनीचांशकसंयुतो'.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Debilitated Navamsha

check_logic.condition: Check if a planet is placed in its debilitated sign in the Navamsha (D9) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the sign placement of the planet in D9 and compare it with the planet's debilitation sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Weakens the planet's ability to deliver results, especially concerning marriage, fortune, and general well-being.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'नीचांशकसंयुतो' (associated with debilitated navamsha).

rules.rule_name: Planet in Inimical Navamsha

check_logic.condition: Check if a planet is placed in an inimical sign in the Navamsha (D9) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the sign placement of the planet in D9 and compare it with planetary friendship/enmity tables.

rules.impact_if_violated: Reduces the planet's strength and beneficence in matters related to the D9 chart.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'अरिनीचांशकसंयुतो' (associated with inimical navamsha).

rules.rule_name: Planet in Exalted Sign

check_logic.condition: Check if a planet is placed in its sign of exaltation in the Rashi (D1) chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the sign placement of the planet and compare it with its exaltation sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Absence of this condition means the planet lacks a significant source of inherent strength and beneficence.
