rules.extraction_notes: Definition of a specific planetary aspect/movement. The text also lists effects of Simhavalokana, but these are interpretations, not validation rules.

rules.rule_name: Planet accompanied by friendly planets

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to give auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is conjoined with or closely associated with planets considered friendly to it.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet's capacity to deliver auspicious results is reduced or compromised if not accompanied by friends.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 78
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'मित्रान्विते' (accompanied by friends) indicating a positive condition for good results.

rules.rule_name: Planet aspected by natural benefics

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's ability to give auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is aspected by natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, well-associated Mercury).

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet's capacity to deliver auspicious results is reduced if not aspected by benefics.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 78
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'शुभैर्दृष्टे' (aspected by benefics) indicating a positive condition for good results.

rules.rule_name: Planet devoid of strength

check_logic.condition: When assessing any planet's ability to deliver its significations.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the planet's overall strength (e.g., Shadbala, Sthana Bala, Kala Bala, Cheshta Bala, Naisargika Bala, Dik Bala).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet will be unable to deliver its full promise, leading to poor or absent results related to its significations and house lordships.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 78
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'बलहीने' (devoid of strength) indicating a negative condition for results.

rules.rule_name: Planet in enemy sign

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's dignity and ability to give auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: Check the planet's sign placement against its natural enemies as defined in classical texts.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet will struggle to give good results, potentially leading to adverse outcomes related to its significations and house lordships.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 78
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'अरिनीचस्थे' (situated in enemy sign) indicating a negative condition for results. 'अरि' means enemy.

rules.rule_name: Planet in debilitation sign

check_logic.condition: When assessing a planet's dignity and ability to give auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: Check the planet's sign placement against its debilitation sign as defined in classical texts.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet is extremely weak and will give very poor or adverse results related to its significations and house lordships, unless there is a cancellation (Neechabhanga).

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Neechabhanga Raja Yoga is present
cancellation_conditions.impact: Debilitation effect is cancelled or reversed, leading to powerful results.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 78
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'अरिनीचस्थे' (situated in debilitation sign) indicating a negative condition for results. 'नीच' means debilitated.

rules.rule_name: Planet afflicted by Sun's rays (Combustion)

check_logic.condition: When assessing any planet's ability to deliver its significations.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is within the specific combustion degrees of the Sun for that planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet loses its independent ability to function, its significations are burnt, suppressed, or become subservient to the Sun's agenda.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If planet is retrograde while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced or modified.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If planet is exalted or in own sign while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced.

rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 78
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'दिनेशकस्पीडिते' (afflicted by the Sun's rays) indicating a negative condition for results.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Maleficence Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing the nature of a planet's Dasa or general influence.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is considered malefic based on its natural disposition (e.g., Saturn, Mars, Sun, Rahu, Ketu) or functional role for the specific Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Dasa periods, leading to erroneous predictions regarding challenges or difficulties.
rules.extraction_notes: Implicitly required for identifying 'malefic' planets mentioned in the definition of Dasachidra.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Eclipse/Affliction Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and influence of a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is combust (within specific degrees of the Sun) or closely conjoined/aspected by Rahu or Ketu.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to account for significant weakening or distortion of planetary results, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Implicitly required for identifying 'eclipsed' planets mentioned in the definition of Dasachidra.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Debilitation Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing the fundamental dignity and strength of a planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its sign of debilitation (Neecha Rashi).

rules.impact_if_violated: Overestimation of a planet's positive influence or underestimation of its challenges, leading to flawed predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Implicitly required for identifying 'depressed' planets mentioned in the definition of Dasachidra.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Ill-Placement Assessment

check_logic.condition: When assessing the general auspiciousness or challenges associated with a planet's placement.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in a Dusthana (6th, 8th, or 12th house) or in an inimical sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of a planet's capacity to deliver favorable or unfavorable results, especially during its Dasa.
rules.extraction_notes: Implicitly required for identifying 'ill-placed' planets mentioned in the definition of Dasachidra.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength Assessment (General)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the results of a planet or the house it rules, specifically the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the overall strength of the planet based on dignity, placement, aspects, and associations.

rules.impact_if_violated: Weak planets give painful results, strong planets give good results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: General principle stated at the beginning of the slokas.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Exaltation

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dignity of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is in its sign of exaltation or a varga belonging to its exaltation house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet does not gain strength from exaltation, reducing its capacity to give good results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for good effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Debilitation

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dignity of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is in its sign of debilitation.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet is significantly weakened, leading to painful results related to its significations and house lordship.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for painful effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Own Sign

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dignity of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is placed in its own sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet does not gain strength from own sign placement, reducing its capacity to give good results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for good effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Friendly Sign

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dignity of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is placed in a sign owned by a friendly planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet does not gain strength from friendly placement, reducing its capacity to give good results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for good effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity - Inimical Sign

check_logic.condition: When assessing the dignity of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is placed in a sign owned by an inimical planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet is weakened by inimical placement, leading to painful results related to its significations and house lordship.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for painful effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary State - Combustion by Sun

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is within the specified degrees of combustion from the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet is eclipsed and loses its capacity to give good results, often leading to painful outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly mentioned as 'eclipsed by the Sun's rays'.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Placement - Dusthana (6th, 8th, 12th Houses)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the placement of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is placed in the 6th, 8th, or 12th house from the Lagna or the house under consideration.

rules.impact_if_violated: Planet's placement in Dusthana houses leads to painful or adverse results related to its significations and house lordship.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly mentioned as 'occupies the 6th, the 8th or the 12th bhava'.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Association - Friendly Planets

check_logic.condition: When assessing the results of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets conjoined with the planet under consideration and determine if they are friendly.

rules.impact_if_violated: Lack of friendly association reduces the positive outcome of the planet.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for good effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Aspect - Benefic Planets

check_logic.condition: When assessing the results of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets aspecting the planet under consideration and determine if they are natural benefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: Lack of benefic aspect reduces the positive outcome of the planet.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for good effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Aspect - Malefic or Unfriendly Planets

check_logic.condition: When assessing the results of the lord of the Rasi under consideration.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets aspecting the planet under consideration and determine if they are natural malefics or unfriendly.

rules.impact_if_violated: Malefic or unfriendly aspects contribute to painful outcomes for the planet's significations.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 77-79
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the conditions for painful effects.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Years for Dasha Calculation (Sun to Saturn)

check_logic.condition: When calculating Dasha periods or other time-based planetary influences that rely on specific planetary years.
check_logic.calculation: Assign the specified years to each planet: Sun=5, Moon=21, Mars=7, Mercury=9, Jupiter=10, Venus=16, Saturn=4.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dasha calculations and timing of events, leading to fundamentally flawed predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from a direct statement of planetary years, which are foundational for many Dasha systems.

rules.rule_name: Sign Years Correspondence to Planetary Lords

check_logic.condition: When determining the 'years' (e.g., Dasha periods) associated with a specific Rasi (sign).
check_logic.calculation: The years assigned to a Rasi must be identical to the years assigned to its planetary lord, as per the established planetary years table (e.g., Sun=5 for Leo, Moon=21 for Cancer, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Rasi Dasha calculations and timing of events, leading to fundamentally flawed predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: Direct statement following the planetary years, indicating how sign years are derived from their lords. This rule relies on the 'Planetary Years for Dasha Calculation' rule.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength Precedes Yoga Effect

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the formation and effects of any astrological yoga (good or bad).
check_logic.calculation: The strength of the planets involved in a yoga must be accurately assessed before determining the yoga's potential and outcome.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of yoga results, leading to flawed promise analysis and potential misinterpretation of the chart's inherent potential.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse २
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'A person attains to a yoga good or bad exactly in accordance with the strength of planetary positions'.

rules.rule_name: Yoga Character Determines Dasa Effect

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the results and experiences during a Dasa period.
check_logic.calculation: The character (good or bad) and strength of the yogas present in the chart directly determine the nature of the experiences and outcomes during the corresponding Dasa periods.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction of Dasa period outcomes, leading to flawed timing analysis and misguidance regarding life events.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse २
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'and the character of the dasa that he will have is dependent on the character of the yoga'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Analysis is Mandatory for All Predictions

check_logic.condition: When making any prediction or analysis about a person's life experiences and events.
check_logic.calculation: A thorough analysis of the relevant Dasa and Antardasa periods is mandatory, as all human experiences (good and evil) are manifested through the operation of Dasas.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete or inaccurate predictions, as the timing and manifestation of all life events are primarily governed by the Dasa system.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse २
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'and all that men experience in the world is comprehended in the good and evil cropping up in the dasas'.

rules.rule_name: Vimshottari Dasha Lord Sequence and Nakshatra Mapping

check_logic.condition: When calculating Vimshottari Dasha, the count of Nakshatras must start from Krittika and be grouped in cycles of nine.
check_logic.calculation: The Dasha lords for the nine Nakshatras in each group must follow the sequence: Sun, Moon, Mars, Rahu, Jupiter, Saturn, Mercury, Ketu, Venus.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assignment of Dasha lords to Nakshatras, leading to fundamental errors in Dasha calculation and timing predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the specific sequence of planetary lords for the Vimshottari Dasha system, starting from Krittika Nakshatra and cycling through groups of nine.

rules.rule_name: Vimshottari Dasha Period Durations

check_logic.condition: When calculating Vimshottari Dasha periods for the Dasha lords defined in the sequence.
check_logic.calculation: The Dasha periods in years must be: Sun (6), Moon (10), Mars (7), Rahu (18), Jupiter (18), Saturn (19), Mercury (17), Ketu (7), Venus (20).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dasha period durations, leading to errors in the length of Dasha periods and subsequent timing predictions.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies the exact duration in years for each planetary Dasha as given in the text. Note that Jupiter's Dasha duration is stated as 18 years in this source, which differs from the commonly accepted 16 years in some other traditions.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Bhava Sandhi is inauspicious

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies Bhava Sandhi (junction of two houses).
check_logic.calculation: Identify the exact degrees of Bhava Sandhi (typically the last few degrees of one house and the first few degrees of the next). Check if a planet's longitude falls within this range.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's significations and its Dasha/Antardasha results will be inauspicious, weak, or unstable.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 6
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the first part of Verse 6, describing conditions for inauspiciousness.

rules.rule_name: Planet associated with malefic planets is inauspicious

check_logic.condition: A planet is associated with malefic planets (e.g., by conjunction, aspect, or house lordship).
check_logic.calculation: Identify natural and functional malefic planets. Determine if the planet in question is conjoined with, aspected by, or otherwise closely associated with these malefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's significations and its Dasha/Antardasha results will be negatively influenced by the malefic association, leading to obstacles or adverse outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 6
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the first part of Verse 6, describing conditions for inauspiciousness.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Rasi Sandhi is inauspicious

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies Rasi Sandhi (junction of two signs).
check_logic.calculation: Identify the exact degrees of Rasi Sandhi (typically the last few degrees of one sign and the first few degrees of the next). Check if a planet's longitude falls within this range.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's significations and its Dasha/Antardasha results will be inauspicious, weak, or unstable, leading to confusion or lack of clarity in its results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 6
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the first part of Verse 6, describing conditions for inauspiciousness.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Lord in auspicious houses from Lagna for auspicious Dasha

check_logic.condition: The lord of the commencing Dasha is placed in the Lagna (1st house), 3rd, 6th, 10th, or 11th house from the natal Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Dasha lord for the current or commencing Dasha. Determine its house placement relative to the natal Lagna. Check if it falls in the 1st, 3rd, 6th, 10th, or 11th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Dasha lord is not in these specified houses, the Dasha may not be as auspicious or may yield mixed results, requiring further analysis of other planetary placements and aspects.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 6
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the second part of Sloka 6, describing conditions for an auspicious Dasha.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Rasi position determines Bhava outcome

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential and outcome of any Bhava
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Moon's Rasi position (in the Bhava under consideration) is classified as favorable or unfavorable according to classical principles. A favorable position promotes prosperity for the Bhava, while an unfavorable position causes damage.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the prosperity or damage related to the Bhava occupied by the Moon.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 9
rules.extraction_notes: This rule mandates checking the Moon's Rasi position (favorable/unfavorable) to determine its impact on the Bhava it occupies. The specific criteria for 'favorable'/'unfavorable' are implied to be known from ancient texts but not detailed in this verse.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength: Shadbala (Six-fold Strength)

check_logic.condition: For a planet to be considered propitious and capable of giving auspicious results.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the Shadbala (six-fold strength) for the planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give auspicious results is significantly reduced, and its promises may not fully materialize.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 4
rules.extraction_notes: The text explicitly mentions 'six-fold strength' (Shadbala) as a requirement for a planet to be propitious, and 'predominant strength' as a general condition.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity: Exaltation

check_logic.condition: For a planet to be considered propitious and inherently strong.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is placed in its sign of exaltation.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's inherent strength and ability to give auspicious results are significantly reduced if not exalted.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 4
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly states 'when they have attained to exaltation' as a condition for being propitious.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity: Benefic Vargas

check_logic.condition: For a planet to be considered propitious and strong across various life areas.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet occupies benefic vargas (e.g., own sign, exaltation sign, friendly sign, Vargottama) in relevant divisional charts (e.g., D9 for marriage, D10 for career).

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give auspicious results in specific areas of life (represented by the divisional chart) is reduced.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 4
rules.extraction_notes: Refers to 'other benefic vargas' (Tungadi Vargopagata) as a condition for a planet to be propitious, implying dignity in divisional charts.

rules.rule_name: Planetary State: Progress Towards Exaltation (Aroha Veerya)

check_logic.condition: For a planet to be considered propitious and gaining strength.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet is in a state of 'Aroha Veerya', meaning it is progressing towards its exaltation sign or degree.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's auspiciousness and its capacity to deliver positive results are reduced if it is not gaining strength towards exaltation.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 4
rules.extraction_notes: The phrase 'in their progress towards exaltation' (Aroha Veeryadhika) describes a specific planetary state indicating increasing strength.

rules.rule_name: Ashtakavarga: Benefic Dots in Bhava

check_logic.condition: For a planet to be considered propitious concerning the bhava it dominates or signifies.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the benefic dots (bindus) in the bhava corresponding to the planet's natural signification or lordship. The number of benefic dots must be more than the average for that specific bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's ability to give auspicious results related to that bhava is diminished if the benefic dots are not above average.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 4
rules.extraction_notes: The text states 'when they have more than the average number of benefic dots correspondingly with the bhavas they dominate' (Bindvadhika), referring to Ashtakavarga strength.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Placement: Auspicious Bhavas (1st, 10th, 11th)

check_logic.condition: For a planet to be considered propitious due to its house placement.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet occupies the 1st (Lagna), 10th (Karma Bhava), or 11th (Labha Bhava) houses.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's general auspiciousness and its capacity to deliver positive results are reduced if not in these favorable houses, though other factors may compensate.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 4
rules.extraction_notes: Explicitly lists 'when they occupy the 10th, the 11th and the first bhavas' (Karmabhavodayasthah) as a condition for a planet to be propitious.

rules.rule_name: Planet owning Mandi's sign produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet is the lord of the Rasi (sign) occupied by Mandi.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the Rasi where Mandi is placed. 2. Identify the planetary lord of that Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'मान्द्रिाशिपतिमान्दिभावगाः' - 'they own the Rasi occupied by Mandi'

rules.rule_name: Planet occupying Mandi's Bhava produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies the same Bhava (house) as Mandi.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the Bhava where Mandi is placed. 2. Check if any planet is also in that Bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'मान्दिभावगाः' - 'they occupy the bhavas containing Mandi'

rules.rule_name: Planet with fewer than average benefic dots produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet has fewer than the average number of benefic dots in Ashtakavarga.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the benefic dots (Sarvashtakavarga or Bhinnashtakavarga for the house it occupies) for the planet. The average is typically 4.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results due to lack of strength/support.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'स्वल्पविन्दुरिपुनीचमूढगाः' - 'associated with fewer than the average number of benefic dots'

rules.rule_name: Planet in an inimical sign produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies a sign that is inimical to its nature.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the natural friendship/enmity relationships between planets and the lord of the sign the planet occupies.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results due to being in an uncomfortable/hostile environment.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'रिपुनीचमूढगाः' - 'in inimical signs'

rules.rule_name: Planet in its sign of debilitation produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies its sign of debilitation (Neecha Rasi).
check_logic.calculation: Refer to classical tables for planetary exaltation and debilitation signs.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results due to extreme weakness and lack of dignity.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If Neechabhanga Raja Yoga is present
cancellation_conditions.impact: Debilitation effect is cancelled or converted to benefic

rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'रिपुनीचमूढगाः' - 'in depressed signs'

rules.rule_name: Combust planet produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet is combust (eclipsed by the Sun).
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the angular distance between the planet and the Sun. If it falls within the specific combustion degrees for that planet, it is combust.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results due to loss of power and visibility.

cancellation_conditions.condition: If planet is retrograde while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced or partially cancelled

cancellation_conditions.condition: If planet is exalted while combust
cancellation_conditions.impact: Combustion effect is reduced

rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'रिपुनीचमूढगाः' - 'eclipsed signs' (interpreted as combustion)

rules.rule_name: Planet in Bhava Sandhi and associated with malefics produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet is located in a Bhava Sandhi (house junction) AND is associated with (conjoined or aspected by) malefic planets.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify Bhava Sandhi (typically 1-2 degrees on either side of the house cusp midpoint). 2. Check if the planet is within this range. 3. Identify natural malefic planets (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury with malefics). 4. Check for conjunctions or aspects from these malefics to the planet in Bhava Sandhi.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results due to weakness from being in a junction and affliction from malefics.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'पापखेटयुतभावसन्धिगा' - 'in a Bhava Sandhi associated with malefic planets'

rules.rule_name: Planet in Rasi Sandhi produces evil

check_logic.condition: A planet is located in a Rasi Sandhi (sign junction).
check_logic.calculation: Identify Rasi Sandhi (typically the last few degrees of a sign and the first few degrees of the next sign, e.g., 29-0 degrees). Check if the planet is within this range.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet produces evil results due to weakness from being in a junction between signs.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from 'राशिसन्धिलवगास्त्वनिष्टदाः' - 'in Rasi Sandhi'

rules.rule_name: Comprehensive Dasha Analysis Factors

check_logic.condition: When predicting the effects of a planetary Dasha.
check_logic.calculation: It is mandatory to consider the following factors for the Dasha lord: its fundamental characteristics (Sangna), its significations related to profession, the effects of its placement in various Bhavas, the effects of its associations (conjunctions) with other planets, the effects of its aspects on other planets and Bhavas, and the effects of yogas it forms (excluding Nabhasa yogas). Additionally, its role as a Bhava lord and its general influence must be integrated into the analysis. Merely considering specific Dasha effects of the planet is insufficient.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete or inaccurate Dasha predictions, leading to a misinterpretation of life events and potential misguidance during the Dasha period.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the directive on comprehensive Dasha analysis, emphasizing the need to consider multiple planetary factors beyond just the specific Dasha effects. The text explicitly states 'all this must be duly assigned' and 'it will not do to mention only what has been described'.

rules.rule_name: Adherence to a Chosen Dasa System's Rules

check_logic.condition: Before commencing any Dasa-based timing analysis.
check_logic.calculation: A specific Dasa system (e.g., Vimshottari, Kalachakra, Ashtottari) must be chosen, and all subsequent Dasa and Bhukti calculations, sequences, and periods must strictly adhere to the rules of that chosen system. Mixing rules from different Dasa systems is not permitted.

rules.impact_if_violated: Inconsistent application of Dasa rules will lead to fundamentally flawed timing predictions and analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, distinction between 'first school' and 'second school' of Dasa calculation
rules.extraction_notes: Inferred from the text presenting two distinct 'schools' of Dasa calculation, each with its own initial Dasa, sequence, and period assignments, implying that one must choose and stick to a system.

rules.rule_name: Initial Mahadasa determined by Moon's Nakshatra

check_logic.condition: When determining the starting Mahadasa for Nakshatra-based Dasa systems (e.g., Vimshottari).
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Nakshatra occupied by the Moon at the time of birth. The lord of this Nakshatra determines the initial Mahadasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect initial Mahadasa leads to completely erroneous Dasa sequence and timing predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, initial mahadasa statement
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the statement 'the initial mahadasa being that of the quarter asterism occupied by the Moon'.

rules.rule_name: Standard Angular Duration of a Nakshatra

check_logic.condition: When calculating the elapsed or remaining portion of a Dasa based on Moon's position in a Nakshatra.
check_logic.calculation: A full Nakshatra spans 13 degrees 20 minutes of arc, which is equivalent to 800 minutes of arc.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Nakshatra duration leads to errors in calculating the balance of Dasa at birth and subsequent Dasa periods.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, 'dividing this by 800 (the number of minutes of a star)'
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the calculation example, where 800 minutes is explicitly stated as 'the number of minutes of a star'.

rules.rule_name: Fixed Dasa Periods for Rasis/Planets within a System

check_logic.condition: When applying any Dasa system for astrological timing.
check_logic.calculation: Each Rasi or planetary lord within a specific Dasa system (e.g., Kalachakra Dasa, Vimshottari Dasa) is assigned a fixed number of years for its Mahadasa period. These periods must be adhered to as per the chosen system.

rules.impact_if_violated: Using incorrect Dasa periods will result in completely inaccurate timing of events.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, examples of '83 years' and '16 years' for specific Dasas
rules.extraction_notes: Inferred from the examples showing specific year assignments (e.g., 83 years for a full cycle in one system, 16 years for Shukra in another).

rules.rule_name: Proportional Calculation of Bhukti Periods

check_logic.condition: When calculating sub-periods (Bhuktis) within a Mahadasa.
check_logic.calculation: Bhukti periods are calculated proportionally based on the Mahadasa period and the assigned Dasa period of the Bhukti lord, relative to the total Dasa cycle period. Formula: Bhukti Period = (Mahadasa Period * Bhukti Lord's Dasa Period) / Total Dasa Cycle Period.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Bhukti calculations will lead to inaccurate timing of sub-events within a Mahadasa.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, 'the bhuktis of the 10 years of मीनगुरुदशा will be (1) मीनगुरु 10 × 10 / 83 years; (2) कुंभशनि 10×10 / 83 years; (3) मकरशनि 7 x 10 / 83 years; (4) धनुर्गुरु 4 x 10 / 83 years; (5) वृश्विवतुःत 9 × 10 / 83 years; (6) तुलाशुक 16×10 / 83 years; (7) कन्याबुध 4×10 / 83 years; (8) तुलाशुक्र 7×10 / 83 years; and (9) वृश्चिककुअ years.'
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted directly from the provided Bhukti calculation examples, which clearly show the proportional formula.

rules.rule_name: Saptavarga Lagna/Sign Lordship Determination

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength and auspiciousness of Dasa periods or planetary placements in divisional charts.
check_logic.calculation: For each of the Saptavargas (D1, D2, D3, D7, D9, D12, D30), identify the planet that owns the sign occupied by the Lagna or a specific planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary strength, dignity, and Dasa period results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 7
rules.extraction_notes: Implied as a prerequisite for assessing the auspiciousness of a Dasa lord based on its Saptavarga ownership.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Natural Friendships Determination

check_logic.condition: Always required for assessing planetary relationships and their combined effects.
check_logic.calculation: Refer to classical tables defining natural friendships and enmities between planets (e.g., Sun's friends are Moon, Mars, Jupiter; enemies are Saturn, Venus).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of planetary relationships, leading to erroneous predictions regarding planetary interactions and Dasa/Antardasa results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 7 (implied)
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by the text's reference to 'friendly planet' in the Lagna. This is a foundational rule for planetary analysis.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Natural Benefic/Malefic Classification

check_logic.condition: Always required for assessing the inherent nature and influence of planets.
check_logic.calculation: Classify planets as natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury with benefics) or natural malefics (Sun, Mars, Saturn, waning Moon, Mercury with malefics).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of a planet's inherent nature, leading to erroneous predictions about its general influence and Dasa/Antardasa results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 7 (implied)
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by the text's reference to 'benefic one' in the Lagna. This is a foundational rule for planetary analysis.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Exaltation Sign Determination

check_logic.condition: Always required for assessing planetary dignity and inherent strength.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the specific zodiac sign where each planet is considered exalted according to classical texts (e.g., Sun in Aries, Moon in Taurus, Mars in Capricorn, Mercury in Virgo, Jupiter in Cancer, Venus in Pisces, Saturn in Libra).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of a planet's dignity and strength, leading to erroneous predictions about its capacity to deliver results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 7 (implied)
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by the text's reference to 'exaltation house' of the Dasa lord. This is a foundational rule for planetary dignity.

rules.rule_name: Trikona House Identification

check_logic.condition: Always required for assessing the auspiciousness and strength of houses, and planetary placements within them.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the 1st, 5th, and 9th houses from the Lagna (or a specific reference house) as Trikona houses.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of house auspiciousness and the nature of planetary placements, leading to flawed interpretations of chart potential.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 7 (implied)
rules.extraction_notes: Implied by the text's reference to 'Trikona house'. This is a foundational rule for house classification.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Mutual Relationship Assessment for Dasha/Bhukti

check_logic.condition: When assessing Dasha and Bhukti results involving two planets.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the mutual relationship (friendly, neutral, inimical) between the two planets based on natural and temporal relationships as per classical texts.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction of Dasha/Bhukti results regarding prosperity or adversity, as mutually friendly planets with strength give good results, while inimical and weak planets give adverse results.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the conditions 'mutually friendly' (अन्योन्यमिष्टग्रहयोः) and 'inimical' (शत्रग्रहौ) for Dasha/Bhukti results.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Six-Fold Strength (Shadbala) Assessment for Dasha/Bhukti

check_logic.condition: When assessing the capacity of planets to deliver results during their Dasha and Bhukti periods.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the six-fold strength (Shadbala) for each planet. This includes Sthana Bala, Dig Bala, Kala Bala, Cheshta Bala, Naisargika Bala, and Drik Bala. Assess if the planets possess 'six fold strength' (पद्मलशालिनोस्तु) or are 'weak' (दुर्बलशालिनौ).

rules.impact_if_violated: Erroneous prediction of Dasha/Bhukti outcomes, as strong planets deliver their results effectively (especially if friendly), while weak planets (especially if inimical) lead to adverse results.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the explicit mention of 'six fold strength' (पद्मलशालिनोस्तु) and 'weak' (दुर्बलशालिनौ) as crucial factors for Dasha/Bhukti results.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord Ishta and Kashta Calculation

check_logic.condition: When determining the results of a planetary Dasa (period).
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the Ishta (auspicious influence) and Kashta (inauspicious influence) values for the planet acting as the Dasa lord, as per Sripatipaddhati Adhyaya 1, Sloka 4.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect determination of Dasa period results, leading to erroneous predictions regarding good and evil experiences.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 10
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the instruction to determine Dasa results based on Ishta and Kashta.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Results - Ishta Predominance

check_logic.condition: When the Ishta portion of a Dasa lord's influence is greatly in excess of its Kashta portion.
check_logic.calculation: Compare Ishta and Kashta values. If Ishta is significantly greater than Kashta, good results are experienced.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to predict good experiences during the Dasa period, leading to an inaccurate assessment of the period's nature.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 11
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the outcome (good) when Ishta is predominant.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Results - Kashta Predominance

check_logic.condition: When the Kashta portion of a Dasa lord's influence is predominant over its Ishta portion.
check_logic.calculation: Compare Ishta and Kashta values. If Kashta is significantly greater than Ishta, evil results are mostly suffered.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to predict evil experiences during the Dasa period, leading to an inaccurate assessment of the period's nature.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 11
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the outcome (evil) when Kashta is predominant.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Results - Ishta and Kashta Even

check_logic.condition: When the Ishta and Kashta portions of a Dasa lord's influence are even.
check_logic.calculation: Compare Ishta and Kashta values. If Ishta is approximately equal to Kashta, mixed results are experienced.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to predict mixed experiences during the Dasa period, leading to an inaccurate assessment of the period's nature.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Sloka 11
rules.extraction_notes: This rule defines the outcome (mixed) when Ishta and Kashta are even.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord Auspiciousness Assessment

check_logic.condition: When predicting effects of a Dasa or Antardasa period.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Dasa/Antardasa lord is auspicious (Shubha) or inauspicious (Ashubha) in the nativity.

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions regarding gains or losses will be fundamentally incorrect, potentially reversed.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, introductory paragraph before Sloka 13
rules.extraction_notes: The text states 'should the Sun he auspicious in the nativity' and 'If the Sun be bad', implying this assessment is mandatory before predicting results.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord Strength Assessment for Effect Attenuation

check_logic.condition: When predicting effects of a Dasa or Antardasa period.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength of the Dasa/Antardasa lord. Specifically, check if the planet is 'void of strength' (Nishbala).

rules.impact_if_violated: The magnitude of predicted effects will be inaccurate; strong effects might be predicted for a weak planet, or vice-versa.
rules.extraction_notes: The text explicitly states 'Where planets are void of strength, the effects though attenuated inake themselves felt at least in dreamy reveries.' This mandates a strength check.

rules.rule_name: Comprehensive Dasa Lord Factor Consideration

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the effects of a Dasa or Antardasa period.
check_logic.calculation: The following factors for the Dasa/Antardasa lord MUST be considered: Rasi position, association with other planets, benefic dots in Ashtakavarga, and occupation of various Vargas (Decanate, Navamsa, etc.).

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa analysis will be incomplete and lack nuance, leading to less accurate or generalized predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, introductory paragraph before Sloka 13
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is extracted from the explicit list of factors that 'will also have to be suitably added on' during Dasa analysis.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Lord's Nature for Specific Undertaking Outcome

check_logic.condition: When predicting the success or failure of a specific undertaking (कार्य) during a particular Dasha.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the planet (Dasha lord) that promotes the specific undertaking. 2. Determine if this planet is a natural benefic (शुभग्रह) or a natural malefic (पापग्रह).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction regarding the success or failure of a specific undertaking during its Dasha.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 21
rules.extraction_notes: This rule establishes a direct correlation between the natural nature (benefic/malefic) of the Dasha lord promoting a specific undertaking and the outcome of that undertaking during its Dasha period. The identification of the 'promoter planet' is a prerequisite step to applying this rule.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength for Future Results from 10th House/Navamsha

check_logic.condition: When assessing the future results (Bhavishyat Phalam) of a planet placed in the 10th house (Karma Rashi) from Lagna or the 10th house in Navamsha (Karma Navamsha Rashi) from Navamsha Lagna.
check_logic.calculation: The planet must be assessed for strength (Balinah). This typically involves evaluating Shadbala, dignity, aspects, and other classical strength parameters.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the planet is not strong, its Dasha (Paka) may not effectively deliver future results, or the results may be weak, delayed, or problematic, despite its placement in the 10th house/Navamsha.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 23
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the term 'बलिनः' (balinah) meaning 'strong' in the context of a planet in the 10th house/Navamsha giving future results. The specific method or threshold for 'strong' is not defined in this verse but is a prerequisite.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Lord Strength Assessment for Malefic Effects

check_logic.condition: When assessing the effects of a planetary Dasha (Paka).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the Dasha lord is 'Balonito' (devoid of strength) at birth. This typically involves a Shadbala calculation.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Dasha lord is devoid of strength, it will cause destruction or negative effects related to the Bhava it influences, especially when triggered by transit.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'बलोनितो' (Balonito) in the Phaladeepika quote. This is a condition for the Dasha lord to produce negative results.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Lord Combustion Check for Malefic Effects

check_logic.condition: When assessing the effects of a planetary Dasha (Paka).
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasha lord is combust (Maudhya) by being within specific degrees of the Sun.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Dasha lord is combust, it will cause destruction or negative effects related to the Bhava it influences, especially when triggered by transit.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'मौढ्यं' (Maudhya) in the Phaladeepika quote. This is a condition for the Dasha lord to produce negative results.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Lord Debilitation Check for Malefic Effects

check_logic.condition: When assessing the effects of a planetary Dasha (Paka).
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasha lord is in its sign of debilitation (Svanicham) at birth.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Dasha lord is debilitated, it will cause destruction or negative effects related to the Bhava it influences, especially when triggered by transit.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'स्वनीचं' (Svanicham) in the Phaladeepika quote. This is a condition for the Dasha lord to produce negative results, based on planetary dignity tables.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Lord in Enemy Sign Check for Malefic Effects

check_logic.condition: When assessing the effects of a planetary Dasha (Paka).
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasha lord is placed in a sign owned by an enemy planet (Ripumandiram) at birth.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Dasha lord is in an enemy's sign, it will cause destruction or negative effects related to the Bhava it influences, especially when triggered by transit.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'रिपुमन्दिरं' (Ripumandiram) in the Phaladeepika quote. This is a condition for the Dasha lord to produce negative results, based on planetary friendship/enmity tables.

rules.rule_name: Dasha Lord Malefic Transit Trigger

check_logic.condition: When a Dasha lord is identified as weak (devoid of strength, combust, debilitated, or in an enemy sign) at birth.
check_logic.calculation: Observe the transit (Charaat) of the Dasha lord. The negative effects promised by the weak Dasha lord are triggered when it transits into a specific Bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: The negative effects related to the Bhava influenced by the weak Dasha lord are activated during its transit through that Bhava.
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'प्राप्तश्च यद्भावमुपैति चारात् तद्भावनाशं कुरुते तदानीम्' in the Phaladeepika quote. This rule connects the promise (weak Dasha lord) with the timing/trigger (transit).

rules.rule_name: Bhava Annihilation due to Inimical Planet in Zero Bindu House

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the potential outcomes for any specific Bhava (e.g., Lagna, 7th house, etc.)
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the lord of the Bhava under consideration. 2. Identify any planet that is 'exceedingly inimical' to this Bhava lord (based on classical planetary friendship/enmity rules). 3. Determine the house occupied by this exceedingly inimical planet. 4. Calculate the benefic dots (bindus) in that house using the Sarvashtakavarga system. 5. If the inimical planet occupies a house with zero benefic dots, the condition is met.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Bhava in question will suffer 'annihilation' (severe detriment or destruction) during the Dasa (planetary period) of the inimical planet.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 29
rules.extraction_notes: This rule combines planetary relationship (enmity), Ashtakavarga strength (zero bindus), and Dasa timing to predict a severe negative outcome for a Bhava. The term 'exceedingly inimical' implies a strong degree of enmity, which might require specific rules for assessing planetary relationships (e.g., natural vs. temporary enmity leading to compound enmity).

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord in Lagna for Dasa Prosperity

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess its prosperity.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa Lord is placed in the Lagna (Ascendant) of the natal chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa may not prove prosperous if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for a prosperous Dasa.

rules.rule_name: Planet Friendly to Dasa Lord in Lagna for Dasa Prosperity

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess its prosperity.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a planet friendly to the Dasa Lord is placed in the Lagna (Ascendant) of the natal chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa may not prove prosperous if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for a prosperous Dasa. Requires knowledge of planetary friendships.

rules.rule_name: Lagna's Varga Owned by Dasa Lord for Dasa Prosperity

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess its prosperity.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa Lord owns the sign that is the Lagna of a relevant divisional chart (varga).

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa may not prove prosperous if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for a prosperous Dasa. 'Varga of this Lagna' is interpreted as the Lagna of a divisional chart.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Planet in Lagna for Dasa Prosperity

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess its prosperity.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a natural benefic planet (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury associated with benefics) occupies the Lagna (Ascendant) of the natal chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa may not prove prosperous if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for a prosperous Dasa.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord in Upachaya from Lagna for Dasa Prosperity

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess its prosperity.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa Lord is placed in an Upachaya house (3rd, 6th, 10th, or 11th) from the natal Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa may not prove prosperous if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for a prosperous Dasa.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Rasi as Dasa Lord's Exaltation Sign for Happy Dasa Effects

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess the happiness of its effects.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Rasi (sign) occupied by the Moon. Check if this Rasi is the exaltation sign of the Dasa Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa effects may not be happy if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for happy Dasa effects.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Rasi as Friendly House to Dasa Lord for Happy Dasa Effects

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess the happiness of its effects.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Rasi (sign) occupied by the Moon. Check if this Rasi is considered a friendly house/sign for the Dasa Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa effects may not be happy if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for happy Dasa effects. Requires knowledge of planetary friendships.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Rasi as Upachaya from Dasa Lord for Happy Dasa Effects

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess the happiness of its effects.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Rasi (sign) occupied by the Moon. Check if this Rasi is in an Upachaya house (3rd, 6th, 10th, or 11th) from the Rasi occupied by the Dasa Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa effects may not be happy if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for happy Dasa effects.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Rasi as Trikona from Dasa Lord for Happy Dasa Effects

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess the happiness of its effects.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Rasi (sign) occupied by the Moon. Check if this Rasi is in a Trikona house (1st, 5th, or 9th) from the Rasi occupied by the Dasa Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa effects may not be happy if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for happy Dasa effects.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Rasi as 7th from Dasa Lord for Happy Dasa Effects

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, to assess the happiness of its effects.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Rasi (sign) occupied by the Moon. Check if this Rasi is in the 7th house from the Rasi occupied by the Dasa Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa effects may not be happy if this condition is not met.
rules.extraction_notes: One of several conditions for happy Dasa effects.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Rasi Not Favorable to Dasa Lord for Unhappy Dasa Effects

check_logic.condition: During a Dasa period, if none of the favorable Moon-Dasa Lord conditions (exaltation, friendly house, Upachaya, Trikona, 7th house) are met.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Rasi occupied by the Moon is NOT the exaltation sign of the Dasa Lord, NOR a friendly house to the Dasa Lord, NOR an Upachaya (3rd, 6th, 10th, 11th) from the Dasa Lord, NOR a Trikona (1st, 5th, 9th) from the Dasa Lord, NOR the 7th house from the Dasa Lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa effects will be far from happy.
rules.extraction_notes: This is the inverse condition explicitly stated in the text for unhappy Dasa effects.

rules.rule_name: Debilitated Planet's Inability to Give Good Results

check_logic.condition: A planet is occupying its sign of debilitation (Neecha Rasi) at birth.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the sign a planet occupies and check if it corresponds to its classical debilitation sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: The debilitated planet will not be competent to produce good effects related to its natural significations, house lordships, and placement.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 28
rules.extraction_notes: Direct statement from the verse: 'A planet occupying its depression at a birth is not competent to produce good effects'.

rules.rule_name: Planet Associated with Debilitated Planet's Inability to Give Good Results

check_logic.condition: A planet is associated (conjoined) with a debilitated planet.
check_logic.calculation: Identify planets that are in conjunction with a planet occupying its sign of debilitation.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet associated with a debilitated planet will also not be competent to produce good effects related to its natural significations, house lordships, and placement.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 28
rules.extraction_notes: Direct statement from the verse: 'nor is another associated therewith'.

rules.rule_name: Debilitated Planet Conjoined with Rahu Causing Harm

check_logic.condition: A debilitated planet is in conjunction with Rahu.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if a planet occupying its sign of debilitation is also conjoined with Rahu.

rules.impact_if_violated: The debilitated planet, when conjoined with Rahu, may actively cause harm related to its significations, house lordships, and the house it occupies.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 28
rules.extraction_notes: Direct statement from the verse: 'If the depressed planet be in conjunction with Rahu, it may do harm'.

rules.rule_name: Planet in a Sign Owned by a Debilitated Planet's Negative Impact

check_logic.condition: A planet occupies a sign whose lord is debilitated.
check_logic.calculation: For any given planet, identify the lord of the sign it occupies. Then, check if that sign lord is debilitated.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet occupying a sign whose lord is debilitated will be negatively affected, potentially unable to produce good effects or causing harm, similar to the debilitated planet itself.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 28
rules.extraction_notes: Interpretation of 'as also the planet occupying a house owned by it' where 'it' refers to the debilitated planet. This implies the house itself is tainted by its debilitated lord, affecting planets within it.

rules.rule_name: Lord of Debilitation Sign's Negative Impact

check_logic.condition: A planet is the lord of the sign where another planet is debilitated.
check_logic.calculation: For each debilitated planet, identify the lord of its debilitation sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet that is the lord of a debilitation sign will also be negatively affected, potentially unable to produce good effects or causing harm, similar to the debilitated planet itself.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 28
rules.extraction_notes: Interpretation of 'and the one owning its depression Rasi'. This refers to the lord of the sign where a planet is debilitated.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Starting Lord Selection by Nakshatra Strength

check_logic.condition: When determining the starting Dasa for a native's chart or a Prashna (query) chart.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify the nakshatra occupied by the Lagna. 2. Identify the nakshatra occupied by the Moon. 3. For Prashna charts, also identify the nakshatra indicated by the person's name and the nakshatra occupied by the Moon at the time of the query. 4. Ascertain the strength of the lords of these relevant nakshatras. 5. The Dasa sequence must commence with the Dasa of the lord of the strongest among these identified nakshatras.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Dasa sequence, leading to entirely erroneous timing predictions for events.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a method for selecting the starting Dasa lord based on the comparative strength of the lords of specific nakshatras (Lagna, Moon, Name, Query Moon). It is fundamental for accurate Dasa calculations.

rules.rule_name: Malefic planet devoid of strength in Ayurdaya division indicates death

check_logic.condition: A natural malefic planet is placed in one of the three cyclic divisions for Ayurdaya purposes AND that planet is devoid of strength (दुर्बलः).
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify natural malefic planets (Sun, Mars, Saturn, Rahu, Ketu, waning Moon, Mercury conjoined with malefics). 2. Identify the three cyclic divisions for Ayurdaya purposes (as per classical texts). 3. Assess the strength of the malefic planet (e.g., using Shadbala or other classical strength assessments) to determine if it is 'devoid of strength'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify this condition may lead to an incorrect prediction of longevity, specifically missing a potential death event during the Dasha of the weak malefic planet.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Verse 26
rules.extraction_notes: This rule identifies a specific planetary condition (malefic, weak, in Ayurdaya division) that predicts death during its Dasha. The definition of 'devoid of strength' (दुर्बलः) requires external interpretation of classical strength assessment methods.

rules.rule_name: Benefic planet devoid of strength in Ayurdaya division indicates well-being

check_logic.condition: A natural benefic planet is placed in one of the three cyclic divisions for Ayurdaya purposes AND that planet is devoid of strength (दुर्बलः).
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify natural benefic planets (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, Mercury unconjoined with malefics). 2. Identify the three cyclic divisions for Ayurdaya purposes (as per classical texts). 3. Assess the strength of the benefic planet (e.g., using Shadbala or other classical strength assessments) to determine if it is 'devoid of strength'.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify this condition may lead to an incorrect prediction of well-being, specifically missing a period of general good fortune during the Dasha of the weak benefic planet.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Verse 26
rules.extraction_notes: This rule identifies a specific planetary condition (benefic, weak, in Ayurdaya division) that predicts well-being during its Dasha. The definition of 'devoid of strength' (दुर्बलः) requires external interpretation of classical strength assessment methods.

rules.rule_name: Badhasthana Identification based on Lagna Type

check_logic.condition: To identify the Badhasthana (troublous house) for any chart.
check_logic.calculation:
Determine the nature of the Lagna (ascendant sign) as moveable, immoveable, or dual. The Badhasthana is identified as follows:
- If Lagna is a Moveable sign (Aries, Cancer, Libra, Capricorn), the 11th house from Lagna is the Badhasthana.
- If Lagna is an Immoveable sign (Taurus, Leo, Scorpio, Aquarius), the 9th house from Lagna is the Badhasthana.
- If Lagna is a Dual sign (Gemini, Virgo, Sagittarius, Pisces), the 7th house from Lagna is the Badhasthana.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of Badhasthana leads to erroneous assessment of inherent obstacles, challenges, and sources of trouble in the chart, affecting all subsequent predictions related to difficulties.
rules.extraction_notes: Definition extracted from the notes section of Verse 31, detailing how to identify Badhasthana based on the Lagna's nature (moveable, immoveable, dual).

rules.rule_name: Badhaka Planet Identification

check_logic.condition: To identify Badhaka planets (planets causing obstacles) in any chart.
check_logic.calculation:
First, identify the Badhasthana (troublous house) as per the Lagna type. Then, identify Badhaka planets based on their relationship to this house:
- The planet owning the Badhasthana is a Badhaka planet.
- Any planet occupying the Badhasthana is a Badhaka planet.
- Any planet occupying a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, or 10th house) from the Badhasthana is a Badhaka planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to correctly identify Badhaka planets will result in misinterpreting the primary sources of obstacles, delays, and significant challenges in a native's life, leading to inaccurate predictions regarding difficulties and struggles.
rules.extraction_notes: Definition extracted from the notes section of Verse 31, detailing how to identify Badhaka planets based on their lordship or occupation of Badhasthana or its Kendras.

rules.rule_name: Shashta-Ashtaka Yoga Identification

check_logic.condition: To identify the presence of Shashta-Ashtaka Yoga (6th and 8th house placement) between any two planets in a chart.
check_logic.calculation: Check if two planets are positioned in the 6th and 8th houses relative to each other. This means if Planet A is in the 6th house from Planet B, then Planet B will be in the 8th house from Planet A, or vice-versa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify this yoga may lead to missing a significant source of challenges, conflicts, sudden events, health issues, and potential danger or exile in a native's life, especially during the Dasha/Antardasha of the involved planets.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule for identifying the planetary configuration known as Shashta-Ashtaka Yoga, which describes two planets in a 6/8 relationship to each other.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Riksha-Sandhi during Dasa

check_logic.condition: A planet's dasa is in progress AND the planet is in Riksha-Sandhi.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet's longitude is within the first degree (0°00' to 0°59') or the last degree (29°00' to 29°59') of any sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native may be afflicted with sorrow and disease during the planet's dasa.
rules.extraction_notes: Riksha-Sandhi refers to the junction of two signs, typically the last degree of a sign and the first degree of the next. The exact degree range for Sandhi is not specified in this verse, but common astrological practice considers the first and last degrees.

rules.rule_name: Planet in Final Degree of Rasi during Dasa (potential for death)

check_logic.condition: A planet's dasa is in progress AND the planet is in the final degree of a Rasi.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the planet's longitude is within the last degree (29°00' to 29°59') of any sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: The planet's dasa may produce death.
rules.extraction_notes: The phrase 'no further than the 30th degree of a Rasi' is interpreted as the planet being in the final degree (29°00' to 29°59') of a sign. This is a common understanding of a planet being in a critical weak state (Mrityu Bhaga or Sandhi) leading to severe outcomes like death, aligning with the context of Riksha-Sandhi mentioned earlier in the verse.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord Natal Strength Assessment

check_logic.condition: Before predicting outcomes of any Dasa period.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the overall strength of the Dasa lord in the natal chart (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, Vargottama, etc.). The text specifically mentions 'बलान्वितश्चेज्जननेऽपि तस्य' (if strong in birth).

rules.impact_if_violated: Predictions about Dasa outcomes will be fundamentally flawed without assessing the planet's inherent strength.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from the Phaladeepika reference: 'बलान्वितश्चेज्जननेऽपि तस्य' (if strong in birth).

rules.rule_name: Benefic in Kendra or Kona Houses

check_logic.condition: When assessing general chart strength or Dasa favorability.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a natural benefic planet (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, unafflicted Mercury) occupies a Kendra (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th) or Kona (1st, 5th, 9th) house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the chart's inherent auspiciousness and potential for positive outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'केन्द्रे वा यदि कोणगेऽतिशुभदः' (When a Kendra or a Kona is occupied by an exceedingly benefic planet).

rules.rule_name: Malefic in Upachaya Houses (3, 6, 11)

check_logic.condition: When assessing general chart strength or Dasa favorability.
check_logic.calculation: Check if a natural malefic planet (Saturn, Mars, Sun, waning Moon, Rahu, Ketu, afflicted Mercury) occupies the 3rd, 6th, or 11th house.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the chart's inherent auspiciousness and potential for overcoming obstacles.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'पापास्त्रिषष्ठायगाः' (and the 3rd, the 6th and the 11th bhavas by malefic ones).

rules.rule_name: Kona Lord Dasa Auspiciousness

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Dasa of a Kona (Trine) lord.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the lord of a Kona house (1st, 5th, 9th). Its Dasa will always be auspicious.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the fundamental auspiciousness of Kona lord Dasas, leading to incorrect Dasa predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'कोणपाः सर्वे शोभनदाः' (All lords of Konas are always auspicious).

rules.rule_name: Trika/Upachaya Lord Dasa Inauspiciousness

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Dasa of a lord of the 3rd, 6th, or 11th house.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the lord of the 3rd, 6th, or 11th house. Even if it is a natural benefic, its Dasa will produce inauspicious results.

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of the fundamental inauspiciousness of 3rd, 6th, 11th lords' Dasas, overriding natural beneficence and leading to incorrect Dasa predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'त्रिवैरिभवपा यद्यप्यनर्थप्रदाः' (The lords of the 3rd, 6th, and 11th houses, even if they are natural benefics, will produce inauspicious results).

rules.rule_name: Kendra Lord Dasa Results

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Dasa of a Kendra (Angle) lord.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the lord of a Kendra house (1st, 4th, 7th, 10th). Its Dasa will produce mixed (good or bad) results depending on its natural benefic/malefic nature and other factors.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the nature of results during a Kendra lord's Dasa, leading to incomplete Dasa predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'केन्द्रेशस्य सत्तोऽसतोऽशुभशुभौ कुर्याद्दशा' (The dasa of the lord of a Kendra, whether benefic or malefic, will produce good or bad results).

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord owns Lagna Rasi

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa period, especially for general life outcomes.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is the lord of the Lagna Rasi (D1).

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa lord's fundamental connection to the native's overall life and self is not established, affecting the interpretation of the Dasa.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the interpretation: 'lord of the dasa is the owner of the Lagna Rasi'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord owns Lagna Hora (D2)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa period, especially for financial matters.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is the lord of the Lagna Hora (D2).

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa lord's connection to the native's wealth and family is not established, affecting the interpretation of the Dasa for these areas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the interpretation: 'lord of the dasa is the owner of the Lagna Hora'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord owns Lagna Decanate (D3)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa period, especially for siblings, courage, and short journeys.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is the lord of the Lagna Decanate (D3).

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa lord's connection to the native's siblings and courage is not established, affecting the interpretation of the Dasa for these areas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the interpretation: 'lord of the dasa is the owner of the Lagna Decanate'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord owns Lagna Navamsa (D9)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa period, especially for marriage, spouse, and dharma.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is the lord of the Lagna Navamsa (D9).

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa lord's connection to the native's marriage and dharma is not established, affecting the interpretation of the Dasa for these crucial areas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the interpretation: 'lord of the dasa is the owner of the Lagna Navamsa'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord owns Lagna Dwadasamsa (D12)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa period, especially for parents and ancestral matters.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is the lord of the Lagna Dwadasamsa (D12).

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa lord's connection to the native's parents and ancestral lineage is not established, affecting the interpretation of the Dasa for these areas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the interpretation: 'lord of the dasa is the owner of the Lagna Dwadasamsa'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord owns Lagna Trimsamsa (D30)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa period, especially for misfortunes, health, and adverse events.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is the lord of the Lagna Trimsamsa (D30).

rules.impact_if_violated: Dasa lord's connection to the native's misfortunes and health is not established, affecting the interpretation of the Dasa for these areas.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Part of the interpretation: 'lord of the dasa is the owner of the Lagna Trimsamsa'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord in Swakshetra (Own Sign)

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa lord's strength and favorability.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is placed in its own sign (swakshetra).

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Dasa lord's inherent strength and ability to deliver its results effectively.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'लम्मादिष्टगृहोपगस्वभवने' (in a swakshetra).

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord in Exaltation

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa lord's strength and favorability.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is placed in its sign of exaltation.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Dasa lord's peak strength and potential for highly favorable results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'तुङ्गे' (in its exaltation).

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord in Friendly House

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa lord's strength and favorability.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is placed in a sign ruled by a planet friendly to it.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Dasa lord's contextual strength and support from its environment.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'सुहृद्धे' (in a friendly house).

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord in Upachaya from Lagna

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa lord's strength and favorability.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is placed in the 3rd, 6th, 10th, or 11th house from the Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Dasa lord's house placement impact, especially regarding growth and achievement over time.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'उपचय (Upachaya) place from the Lagna'.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Lord Aspected by Benefic/Friendly Planet

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a Dasa lord's strength and favorability.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord receives an aspect from a natural benefic planet or a planet friendly to the Dasa lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Dasa lord's external support and auspicious influences, leading to misjudgment of Dasa outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'शुभमित्रवीक्षणयुतस्तत्पाकभुक्तौ शुभम्' (has the aspect on it of a benefic or a friendly planet, its dasa and bhukti will be favourable).

rules.rule_name: Strong Dasa Lord in Exaltation or Upachaya

check_logic.condition: When assessing the prosperity and positive outcomes of a Dasa period.
check_logic.calculation: Check if the Dasa lord is strong (e.g., high Shadbala, Vargottama, etc.) AND is placed in its exaltation sign OR in an Upachaya house (3rd, 6th, 10th, 11th) from Lagna.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the Dasa period's potential for prosperity and success, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Derived from 'बलयुतास्तेषां दशायां शुभम्' (strong... their dasa will be prosperous) and 'तुङ्गाद्योपचयेषु' (exaltation or other such Upachaya places).

rules.rule_name: Utpanna Nakshatra Dasha System Activation

check_logic.condition: The Rasi containing the Utpanna Nakshatra is stronger than any other Rasi referred to in preceding slokas (as potential Dasha starting points).
