check_logic.calculation: Compare the strength of the Rasi housing the Utpanna Nakshatra with other relevant Rasis. The method of 'strength' comparison (e.g., Shadbala, Varga Bala, etc.) must be applied consistently based on classical principles.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to correctly identify the Dasha starting point based on Rasi strength can lead to the selection of an incorrect Dasha system, resulting in entirely erroneous timing predictions for all life events.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule dictates a conditional selection of the Dasha system. The specific 'preceding slokas' and the precise definition of 'stronger' are not detailed in the provided text but are implied to be known from the broader context of the book.

rules.rule_name: General Malefic Tendency at Dasha End

check_logic.condition: Analyzing the end portion of any planetary Dasha period.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the last segment (e.g., last 1/9th, last few months/years) of a planet's Dasha period.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to consider this general tendency may lead to overlooking potential difficulties, challenges, or negative events that commonly manifest at the conclusion of Dasha periods.
rules.extraction_notes: This is a general interpretive rule about the nature of Dasha endings, suggesting a tendency towards negative outcomes.

rules.rule_name: Critical Malefic Effects at End of Specific Dasas (Utpanna, Adhana, Kshema, Maha)

check_logic.condition: Analyzing the end portion of Utpanna, Adhana, Kshema, or Maha Dasha periods.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the last segment of these specific Dasha periods. The text explicitly states 'death-giving results' and 'three types of afflictions and five types of troubles' occur at their conclusion.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to consider this rule can lead to missing predictions of severe afflictions, life-threatening events, or even death at the end of these specific Dasha periods, regardless of the native's overall lifespan prognosis.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is a more severe and specific instance of the general rule from Sloka 34, applying to particular Dasha systems. The full definition of how to ascertain Utpanna, Adhana, Kshema, and Maha nakshatras/dasas is partially given in Sloka 35 but requires further context from the book.

rules.rule_name: Dasa Tally for Demise Timing Confirmation

check_logic.condition: When predicting the timing of demise.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the end points (years, months, days) of Dasas starting from Janmarksha (Moon's Nakshatra), Utpanna (5th Nakshatra from Janmarksha), Adhana (8th Nakshatra from Janmarksha), and Kshema (4th Nakshatra from Janmarksha). Check if the calculated Dasa end points (years, months, etc.) are identical or tally for any of these cycles.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of demise prediction due to failure to confirm Dasa tallying.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from a specific predictive technique for longevity, requiring a check of Dasa cycle end points.

rules.rule_name: Demise Timing for Short-Lived Individuals

check_logic.condition: When the native is determined to have a short life span (promise stage).
check_logic.calculation: The demise is predicted to occur at the close of the 3rd Dasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of demise prediction for short-lived individuals.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from a specific predictive technique for longevity, contingent on prior determination of life span.

rules.rule_name: Demise Timing for Medium-Lived Individuals

check_logic.condition: When the native is determined to have a medium life span (promise stage).
check_logic.calculation: The demise is predicted to occur at the close of the 5th Dasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of demise prediction for medium-lived individuals.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from a specific predictive technique for longevity, contingent on prior determination of life span.

rules.rule_name: Demise Timing for Long-Lived Individuals

check_logic.condition: When the native is determined to have a long life span (promise stage).
check_logic.calculation: The demise is predicted to occur at the close of the 7th Dasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of demise prediction for long-lived individuals.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from a specific predictive technique for longevity, contingent on prior determination of life span.

rules.rule_name: Niryana Dasa Calculation for Daytime Birth

check_logic.condition: When predicting the timing of demise for a person born during daytime.
check_logic.calculation: Sum the precise longitudes (degrees, minutes, seconds) of the Sun and Saturn. Identify the Nakshatra and its specific portion (pada) that corresponds to this total longitude. This identified Nakshatra marks the Niryana Dasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the Dasa indicating exit from the world.
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from a specific calculation method for determining the Dasa of exit from the world, applicable only for daytime births.

rules.rule_name: Karaka's Sign Strength in Rasi/Vargas (Benefic Influence)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for good results related to a Karaka, the strength of the sign occupied by the Karaka in Rasi and relevant Vargas must be validated.
check_logic.calculation: A sign occupied by a Karaka in Rasi or any Varga (Hora, Drekkana, Saptamsa, etc.) is considered strong if it is associated with or aspected by benefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the Karaka's potential for good results, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 39
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the condition for predicting good results based on sign strength in Rasi and Vargas.

rules.rule_name: Karaka's Sign Weakness in Rasi/Vargas (Malefic Influence/Dignity)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for evil results related to a Karaka, the weakness of the sign occupied by the Karaka in Rasi and relevant Vargas must be validated.
check_logic.calculation: A sign occupied by a Karaka in Rasi or any Varga (Hora, Drekkana, Saptamsa, etc.) is considered weak if it is the debilitation sign of the Karaka, an inimical sign for the Karaka, or if it is occupied by or aspected by a malefic planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the Karaka's potential for evil results, leading to inaccurate predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 39
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the condition for predicting evil results based on sign weakness in Rasi and Vargas.

rules.rule_name: Chakra Dasha Special Results Condition (Moon's Nakshatra Lord Influence)

check_logic.condition: When applying Chakra Dasha, validate if the Moon is aspected by or conjoined with the lord of the Nakshatra division it occupies to determine if special results apply.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the Nakshatra division ('tara-amsha') occupied by the Moon. Identify the lord of that Nakshatra division. Check if the Moon is aspected by or conjoined with this lord.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of Chakra Dasha results, potentially missing 'special results'.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the introductory line to the 'Mahadasha Vishesha' section, describing a condition for 'special results' in Chakra Dasha. The term 'tara-amsha' implies a Nakshatra division.

rules.rule_name: Planets in 6th or 8th from each other during Dasa/Bhukti

check_logic.condition: When analyzing Dasa and Bhukti periods of planets.
check_logic.calculation: Identify if the Dasa lord and Bhukti lord are in the 6th or 8th house from each other in the birth chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify potential periods of loss of position or life during Dasa/Bhukti analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 54
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific planetary relationship that becomes active during their Dasa/Bhukti periods, leading to specific adverse outcomes (loss of place or life).

rules.rule_name: Two weak planets in same bhava during Dasa/Antara for death prediction

check_logic.condition: When analyzing Dasa and Antara periods, especially for longevity or critical life events.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify planets occupying the same bhava. 2. Assess the strength of these planets (e.g., using Shadbala or other dignity rules). 3. If both planets are weak and their Dasa/Antara is running, this condition applies.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to identify potential periods of death or severe life-threatening events during Dasa/Antara analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 54
rules.extraction_notes: This rule combines planetary placement, strength assessment, and timing to predict a critical event (death). The definition of 'weak' is not specified in this verse but implies a general lack of strength/dignity.

rules.rule_name: Antardasha Duration Calculation Method

check_logic.condition: When calculating the duration of an Antardasha within a Mahadasa.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Multiply the years of the Mahadasa by the years of the planet whose Antardasha is required. 2. The product, when divested of the figure in the unit's place, represents the months of the Antardasha. 3. Multiply the figure in the unit's place (omitted in step 2) by 3. The result will be the number of days forming part of the Antardasha.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect Antardasha durations, leading to erroneous timing of events and predictions.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita Sloka 61
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific, non-negotiable method for calculating the duration of Antardashas, which is fundamental for accurate timing in astrological predictions.

rules.rule_name: Native's Ayus Karakas (House Lords)

check_logic.condition: When assessing the native's longevity (Ayus).
check_logic.calculation: Identify the lords of the 3rd house and the 8th house from the Lagna. These two planets are the Ayus Karakas for the native.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of primary significators for native's longevity, leading to erroneous analysis.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines Ayus Karakas based on house lordship from Lagna.

rules.rule_name: Father Karaka Selection

check_logic.condition: When assessing matters related to the father.
check_logic.calculation: Compare the strength (Veerya) of the Sun and Venus. The planet with greater strength is considered the primary Karaka for the father.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the primary significator for the father, leading to erroneous analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, Verse 39 (citing Jaimini and Neelakantha)
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the clarification provided by Jaimini and Neelakantha, emphasizing strength comparison between Sun and Venus for father.

rules.rule_name: Mother Karaka Selection

check_logic.condition: When assessing matters related to the mother.
check_logic.calculation: Compare the strength (Veerya) of the Moon and Mars. The planet with greater strength is considered the primary Karaka for the mother.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the primary significator for the mother, leading to erroneous analysis.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, Verse 39 (citing Jaimini and Neelakantha)
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the clarification provided by Jaimini and Neelakantha, emphasizing strength comparison between Moon and Mars for mother.

rules.rule_name: Soola Rasi Determination for Mrityuda/Niryana

check_logic.condition: When calculating Soola Dasa for predicting death (Mrityuda) or the Niryana (demise) of a particular relative.
check_logic.calculation: Identify the relevant Karaka. The Soola Rasi is determined by comparing the strength of the 8th house from the Karaka's placement and the 7th house from the Karaka's placement. The stronger of these two houses becomes the Soola Rasi.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the Soola Rasi, leading to erroneous Soola Dasa calculations and inaccurate timing of death or demise.
rules.extraction_notes: Defines the method to determine Soola Rasi, which is crucial for Soola Dasa calculations.

rules.rule_name: Karaka's House/Planet Strength Assessment for Auspiciousness/Inauspiciousness

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the auspiciousness or inauspiciousness of results related to a Karaka or the house it signifies/occupies.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the strength (Veerya) of the Karaka's house or the Karaka planet itself. Check for benefic aspects (Shubha Drishti) on the Karaka's house/planet. Check for debilitation (Neecha), placement in an enemy sign (Ari), or conjunction with malefics (Papa Yuta) for the Karaka's house/planet. Auspicious results are indicated by strength and benefic aspects. Inauspicious results are indicated by debilitation, enemy sign placement, or malefic conjunctions.

rules.impact_if_violated: Inaccurate assessment of the Karaka's influence, leading to incorrect predictions regarding happiness or misfortune related to the significations of that Karaka.
rules.extraction_notes: General rule for assessing the quality of a Karaka's placement and aspects.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Planet Dasa Effect Timing: Bhava Lordship

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Dasa (planetary period) of a benefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: The effects related to the bhava(s) the benefic planet presides over (its lordship) manifest at the beginning of its Dasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of bhava-related events and outcomes during a benefic planet's Dasa.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 59
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Dasa effects timing for benefic planets, specifically for effects due to bhava lordship.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Planet Dasa Effect Timing: Varga Position

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Dasa (planetary period) of a benefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: The effects related to the benefic planet's position in the several vargas (divisional charts) manifest in the middle of its Dasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of varga-related events and outcomes during a benefic planet's Dasa.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 59
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Dasa effects timing for benefic planets, specifically for effects due to varga position.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Planet Dasa Effect Timing: Aspects

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Dasa (planetary period) of a benefic planet.
check_logic.calculation: The effects related to the aspects of other planets upon the benefic planet manifest at the end of its Dasa.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of aspect-related events and outcomes during a benefic planet's Dasa.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 59
rules.extraction_notes: Rule extracted from the description of Dasa effects timing for benefic planets, specifically for effects due to aspects from other planets.

rules.rule_name: Moon's Strength for Mahadasa Results

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the results of Moon's Mahadasa
check_logic.calculation: Assess the Moon's overall strength. This includes factors like Paksha Bala, Dig Bala, and other components of Shadbala, as the text refers to a general 'check' on strength.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the Moon's strength is compromised or insufficient, the positive effects of its Mahadasa (access to sacred knowledge, fascination over females, acquisition of women, wealth, lands, flowers, cloths, ornaments, perfumes, and valuable objects) will be diminished or reversed. Instead, the person will experience poverty and suffer from wind-related diseases.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 73
rules.extraction_notes: Rule derived from the conditional statement 'but if there be anything to check the Moon's strength', which indicates that Moon's strength is a critical validation factor for the manifestation of its Mahadasa results.

rules.rule_name: Mrityu Dasa Malefic Inimical Planet Condition

check_logic.condition: When predicting the timing of death using Mrityu Dasa of an inimical planet.
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet whose Dasa/Bhukti/Antara is being considered for Mrityu Dasa is both inimical (e.g., to Lagna, Lagna Lord, or other relevant significators of life) and a natural or functional malefic.

rules.impact_if_violated: The prediction of death during the Dasa of an inimical planet is not confirmed and may be unreliable if the planet is not also malefic, leading to potential misinterpretation or incorrect timing.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 58
rules.extraction_notes: This rule specifies a critical condition for validating the prediction of death through Mrityu Dasa. It highlights the necessity of confirming the malefic nature of an inimical planet for this specific prediction to be considered certain.

rules.rule_name: Mahadasha Phala Timing - Moon's Dasha

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the results and timing of events during the Moon's Mahadasha.
check_logic.calculation: The effects due to the bhava (house) over which the Moon presides (i.e., the house where Cancer is located in the birth chart) will manifest in the commencement of its Mahadasha. The effects due to the character of the sign representing the bhava where Moon is placed, as well as to the Moon's own position, will be seen in the middle of the Mahadasha. The effects due to the aspect on the Moon of other planets and what affects the part of the body denoted by the bhava will appear towards the end of the Mahadasha.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect timing of predicted events within the Moon's Mahadasha, leading to inaccurate predictions regarding when specific types of results will manifest.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes the temporal distribution of results within the Moon's Mahadasha based on different astrological factors.

rules.rule_name: Rahu Dasha Auspiciousness based on Benefic Association and Auspicious House Conjunction

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the results of Rahu's Dasha.
check_logic.calculation: Check Rahu's conjunction/aspects with natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, well-placed Mercury) and its placement in auspicious houses (Kendra, Trikona, 2nd, 11th). If Rahu is associated with benefics AND placed in auspicious houses, its Dasha is considered auspicious.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect prediction of Rahu Dasha results, leading to misguidance regarding life events during that period. Failure to identify periods of potential prosperity or adversity.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule provides a critical condition for determining the auspiciousness of Rahu's Dasha. It implies that if these conditions are not met, the Dasha will likely yield malefic results as described in other parts of the text. This is a non-negotiable check for Dasha analysis involving Rahu.

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Dignity Assessment for Rashi Confirmation

check_logic.condition: When evaluating the strength and impact of a planet's placement in the Rashi chart.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the planet's dignity (exaltation, debilitation, own sign, etc.) in the Rashi chart. Subsequently, determine the planet's dignity in the Navamsa (D9) chart. These two dignities must be considered together to understand the planet's final disposition and modify the Rashi chart's indications.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incomplete or inaccurate assessment of a planet's true strength and potential, leading to erroneous predictions regarding its significations (e.g., children, brothers, wealth for Mars, as illustrated by the verse).
rules.extraction_notes: This rule is inferred from the verse which provides specific interpretations based on a planet's dignity in both Rashi and Navamsa, highlighting the necessity of considering both for accurate assessment of a planet's promise. The verse demonstrates that a planet's dignity in the Navamsa can significantly alter or confirm the effects indicated by its Rashi dignity.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter exalted in Rashi but debilitated in Navamsa

check_logic.condition: Jupiter is in its exaltation sign in the Rashi chart AND Jupiter is in its debilitation sign in the Navamsa chart.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify Jupiter's sign in the Rashi chart. 2. Identify Jupiter's sign in the Navamsa chart. 3. Check if the Rashi sign corresponds to Jupiter's exaltation. 4. Check if the Navamsa sign corresponds to Jupiter's debilitation.

rules.impact_if_violated: During Jupiter's Dasha, it brings danger through robbers, foes, and rulers, hatred from wife and sons, and general bad luck.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific dignity state for Jupiter across Rashi and Navamsa, indicating a significant reduction in its positive potential despite Rashi exaltation.

rules.rule_name: Jupiter debilitated in Rashi but exalted in Navamsa

check_logic.condition: Jupiter is in its debilitation sign in the Rashi chart AND Jupiter is in its exaltation sign in the Navamsa chart.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify Jupiter's sign in the Rashi chart. 2. Identify Jupiter's sign in the Navamsa chart. 3. Check if the Rashi sign corresponds to Jupiter's debilitation. 4. Check if the Navamsa sign corresponds to Jupiter's exaltation.

rules.impact_if_violated: During Jupiter's Dasha, it secures royal favor, material comforts, learning, wisdom, fame, wealth, influence, and potentially even lordship of a country.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific dignity state for Jupiter across Rashi and Navamsa, indicating a significant enhancement of its positive potential despite Rashi debilitation.

rules.rule_name: Saturn's Rashi Exaltation and Navamsa Debilitation

check_logic.condition: Saturn is in its exaltation sign in the Rashi chart AND in its debilitation sign in the Navamsa chart.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify Saturn's sign in the Rashi (D1) chart. 2. Identify Saturn's sign in the Navamsa (D9) chart. 3. Check if Saturn is in Libra (exaltation) in D1. 4. Check if Saturn is in Aries (debilitation) in D9.

rules.impact_if_violated: Not applicable, this describes a specific planetary configuration and its effects. If this condition is met, the described effects are expected.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule combines planetary dignity assessment across Rashi and Navamsa charts for Saturn, impacting Dasa results.

rules.rule_name: Saturn's Rashi Debilitation and Navamsa Exaltation

check_logic.condition: Saturn is in its debilitation sign in the Rashi chart AND in its exaltation sign in the Navamsa chart.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Identify Saturn's sign in the Rashi (D1) chart. 2. Identify Saturn's sign in the Navamsa (D9) chart. 3. Check if Saturn is in Aries (debilitation) in D1. 4. Check if Saturn is in Libra (exaltation) in D9.

rules.impact_if_violated: Not applicable, this describes a specific planetary configuration and its effects. If this condition is met, the described effects are expected.
rules.extraction_notes: This rule combines planetary dignity assessment across Rashi and Navamsa charts for Saturn, impacting Dasa results.

rules.rule_name: Mercury in Exaltation Sign but Debilitation Navamsa

check_logic.condition: When Mercury is placed in its sign of exaltation in the Rashi (D1) chart, but simultaneously occupies its sign of debilitation in the Navamsa (D9) chart.
check_logic.calculation: 1. Determine Mercury's Rashi (D1) sign. Check if it is Virgo (Mercury's exaltation sign). 2. Determine Mercury's Navamsa (D9) sign. Check if it is Pisces (Mercury's debilitation sign). Both conditions must be true.

rules.impact_if_violated: The native will experience incompetency for work and become indigent, particularly during Mercury's Dasa period.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 143
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific combination of Rashi dignity (exaltation) and Navamsa dignity (debilitation) for Mercury, leading to significant negative outcomes.

rules.rule_name: Strength of Benefic Planets for Auspicious House Results

check_logic.condition: When assessing the potential for auspicious results (e.g., happiness) from benefic planets placed in Trikona (1st, 5th, 9th), 2nd, or 11th houses.
check_logic.calculation: Assess the overall strength (e.g., Shadbala, Avasthas, positional strength, etc.) of the benefic planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: Benefic planets, even if well-placed, will not deliver their full potential for happiness or auspicious results if they lack sufficient strength.
rules.extraction_notes: The verse explicitly states that benefic planets must be 'strong' (valino) to produce 'much happiness' when occupying Trikona, 2nd, or 11th houses. This implies strength is a prerequisite for the full positive outcome.

rules.rule_name: Venus: Rashi Exaltation & Navamsa Debilitation State

check_logic.condition: When assessing the strength, dignity, and potential outcomes of Venus, particularly concerning wealth and status.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Venus's sign in the Rashi chart. Identify Venus's sign in the Navamsa (D9) chart. The rule applies if Venus is in its exaltation sign in the Rashi chart (e.g., Pisces) AND in its debilitation sign in the Navamsa chart (e.g., Virgo).

rules.impact_if_violated: Misinterpretation of Venus's true strength and potential for significant financial and status loss during its Dasa period, despite apparent Rashi strength.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita, Verse 168
rules.extraction_notes: This rule describes a specific, complex dignity state for Venus that combines Rashi exaltation with Navamsa debilitation, leading to adverse outcomes in its Dasa. It highlights the importance of cross-divisional dignity assessment.

rules.rule_name: Planetary strength and Rashi dignity for benefic Dasha results

check_logic.condition: When assessing the benefic potential of a planet's Dasha
check_logic.calculation: A planet must possess full strength (e.g., Shadbala) AND be in its exaltation sign in the Rashi chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of benefic Dasha results; overestimation of positive outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 170
rules.extraction_notes: This rule establishes a baseline for strong benefic Dasha results based on Rashi dignity and overall strength.

rules.rule_name: Planetary weakness and Rashi dignity for malefic Dasha results

check_logic.condition: When assessing the malefic potential of a planet's Dasha
check_logic.calculation: A planet must be devoid of strength (e.g., low Shadbala) AND be in its depression sign in the Rashi chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of malefic Dasha results; underestimation of negative outcomes or lack of positive outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 170
rules.extraction_notes: This rule establishes a baseline for weak malefic Dasha results based on Rashi dignity and overall weakness.

rules.rule_name: Planetary dignity in Navamsa and inimical Rashi placement for malefic Dasha results

check_logic.condition: When assessing the malefic potential of a planet's Dasha, especially considering divisional and Rashi relationships.
check_logic.calculation: A planet must be in its depression Navamsa AND be placed in an inimical sign in the Rashi chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of malefic Dasha results; missing a significant source of negative outcomes.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3 By Vaidyanatha Dikshita 170
rules.extraction_notes: This rule highlights the combined malefic influence of debilitation in Navamsa and placement in an inimical sign in Rashi for Dasha predictions.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Dignity and Benefic Aspect for Auspicious Dasha Results

check_logic.condition: When assessing the quality of a planet's Dasha or Antardasha.
check_logic.calculation: A planet gives excellent Dasha results if it is in a friendly sign, exaltation sign, or its own sign, AND is aspected by benefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the auspiciousness of a Dasha or Antardasha period.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the first part of the Sanskrit verse: 'मित्रोच्चभागप्तहिताः शुभदृष्टियुक्ताः श्रेष्ठां दशां विदधति स्ववयःसु खेटाः ॥'

rules.rule_name: Planetary Debilitation, Enemy Sign, Combustion, Malefic Association for Inauspicious Dasha Results

check_logic.condition: When assessing the quality of a planet's Dasha or Antardasha.
check_logic.calculation: A planet gives inauspicious Dasha results if it is in debilitation, an enemy sign, an enemy navamsha, combust, or associated with malefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect assessment of the inauspiciousness of a Dasha or Antardasha period.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the second part of the Sanskrit verse: 'नीचशत्रुगृहं प्राप्ताः शत्रुनिम्नांशसूर्यगाः । त्रिवर्णाः पापसंचन्धा दशां कुर्युरशोभनाम् ॥'

rules.rule_name: Bhava Destruction by Maraka Lords from Bhava in Dasha/Antardasha

check_logic.condition: When analyzing the Dasha or Antardasha of a planet that is the lord of the 2nd or 7th house from any specific Bhava.
check_logic.calculation: In the Dasha or Antardasha of the lord of the 2nd or 7th house from a particular Bhava, destruction of that Bhava will occur. The nature and extent of destruction are influenced by planets occupying, aspecting, or being Karakas of that Bhava.

rules.impact_if_violated: Failure to predict potential destruction or significant negative events related to a specific Bhava during relevant Dasha periods.
rules.extraction_notes: Extracted from the third part of the Sanskrit verse: 'तत्तद्भावार्थकामेशदशास्वन्तर्दशासु च । तत्चद्भावविनाशः स्यात् तद्युक्तेक्षितकारकैः'. This rule applies the concept of Maraka houses (2nd and 7th) not just from the Lagna, but from any Bhava, indicating potential destruction to that specific Bhava.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Exaltation Status Check

check_logic.condition: To assess the quality of a planet's Dasa (planetary period).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its sign of exaltation in the Rasi (D1) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa of the planet will not be considered 'full/complete' or 'Sampurna', reducing its auspiciousness and beneficial results.
rules.extraction_notes: Exaltation is a primary factor for a 'full/complete' or 'Sampurna' Dasa, indicating strong positive results.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Debilitation Status Check

check_logic.condition: To assess the quality of a planet's Dasa (planetary period).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed in its sign of debilitation (depression) in the Rasi (D1) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa of the planet will be considered 'empty' or 'untoward', leading to significant negative results, loss of wealth, and health issues.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, Sloka 170; Gargi; Gunakara
rules.extraction_notes: Debilitation is a primary factor for an 'empty' or 'untoward' Dasa, indicating severe negative outcomes.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Deepest Exaltation (Paramochcha) Check

check_logic.condition: To assess the highest possible quality of a planet's Dasa (Sampurna).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed at its deepest exaltation degree (Paramochcha) in the Rasi (D1) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: Even if exalted, the Dasa will not achieve the peak 'Sampurna' quality if the planet is not at its deepest exaltation degree, slightly reducing the magnitude of positive results.
rules.extraction_notes: Gargi specifies 'Paramochcha' for the highest 'Sampurna' Dasa, implying a more precise check than just sign exaltation.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Deepest Debilitation (Paramaneechasth) Check

check_logic.condition: To assess the most untoward quality of a planet's Dasa (Anishta Phala).
check_logic.calculation: Determine if the planet is placed at its deepest debilitation degree (Paramaneechasth) in the Rasi (D1) chart.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa will be of the most 'Anishta Phala' (untoward) quality, leading to severe negative results, diseases, and misfortunes.
rules.extraction_notes: Gargi specifies 'Paramaneechasth' for 'Anishta Phala' Dasa, implying a more precise check than just sign debilitation.

rules.rule_name: Planetary Strength Assessment (General)

check_logic.condition: To assess the general strength of a planet, which directly impacts the quality of its Dasa.
check_logic.calculation: Calculate the planet's overall strength (e.g., using Shadbala or other classical methods). Determine if it possesses 'full strength', 'some strength', or is 'devoid of all strengths'.

rules.impact_if_violated: If the planet lacks strength, its Dasa will be 'empty' or 'mixed', leading to reduced or negative results. If it has full strength, the Dasa will be 'full/complete' or 'Purna', yielding auspicious results.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, Sloka 170; Gargi; Gunakara; Maravali
rules.extraction_notes: Multiple sources emphasize planetary strength (Sarvabalairupetasya, Veeryopetasya, Sampurna Veerya) as a primary factor for Dasa quality. The text does not specify the exact calculation method or numerical thresholds for 'full strength' etc., but mandates the assessment.

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Dignity - Inimical Sign Check

check_logic.condition: To assess the quality of a planet's Dasa, particularly for untoward results.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the sign placement of the planet in the Navamsa (D9) chart. Check if this Navamsa sign is inimical to the planet.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa quality will be 'untoward' or 'Anishta Phala', leading to negative results, diseases, and misfortunes, even if the planet is otherwise well-placed in the Rasi chart.
rules.classical_reference: Jataka Parijata Vol 3, Sloka 170; Gargi; Gunakara
rules.extraction_notes: Navamsa placement in an inimical sign is explicitly mentioned as a cause for untoward Dasa results, highlighting the importance of divisional charts.

rules.rule_name: Navamsa Dignity - Own/Friendly Sign Check

check_logic.condition: To assess the quality of a planet's Dasa, contributing to positive results.
check_logic.calculation: Determine the sign placement of the planet in the Navamsa (D9) chart. Check if this Navamsa sign is the planet's own sign or a friendly sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa quality will be reduced from 'Sreshta' (excellent) if the planet is not in its own or a friendly Navamsa sign, diminishing the auspiciousness of its period.
rules.extraction_notes: Placement in own or friendly Navamsa contributes to a strong and auspicious Dasa, enhancing beneficial outcomes.

rules.rule_name: Benefic Aspect on Planet Check

check_logic.condition: To assess the quality and beneficence of a planet's Dasa.
check_logic.calculation: Identify natural benefics (Jupiter, Venus, waxing Moon, unafflicted Mercury). Check if the planet whose Dasa is being assessed is aspected by any of these benefics.

rules.impact_if_violated: The Dasa quality will be reduced from 'Sreshta' (excellent) if the planet is not aspected by benefics. Negative effects might be unmitigated or even amplified without benefic support.
rules.extraction_notes: Benefic aspects are mentioned as contributing to an excellent Dasa ('Sreshta Dasa'), indicating their importance in enhancing positive results and mitigating negative ones.

rules.rule_name: Aries Lagna: Deha Sign is Aries, Lord is Mars

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a chart with Aries (Mesha) ascendant.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Mars as the Deha Lord for Aries ascendant, as Aries (स्वयं देहो) is the Deha sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the planet governing the physical body/constitution for the native with Aries ascendant.
rules.extraction_notes: Deha sign and its lord are identified for Aries Lagna based on the text 'मेषस्य तु स्वयं देहो'.

rules.rule_name: Aries Lagna: Jiva Sign is Sagittarius, Lord is Jupiter

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a chart with Aries (Mesha) ascendant.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Jupiter as the Jiva Lord for Aries ascendant, as Sagittarius (धनुर्जीवः) is the Jiva sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the planet governing the soul/dharma for the native with Aries ascendant.
rules.extraction_notes: Jiva sign and its lord are identified for Aries Lagna based on the text 'धनुर्जीवः प्रकीर्तितः'.

rules.rule_name: Taurus Lagna: Deha Sign is Capricorn, Lord is Saturn

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a chart with Taurus (Vrishabha) ascendant.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Saturn as the Deha Lord for Taurus ascendant, as Capricorn (मकरो देहो) is the Deha sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the planet governing the physical body/constitution for the native with Taurus ascendant.
rules.extraction_notes: Deha sign and its lord are identified for Taurus Lagna based on the text 'वृषस्य मकरो देहो'.

rules.rule_name: Taurus Lagna: Jiva Sign is Gemini, Lord is Mercury

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a chart with Taurus (Vrishabha) ascendant.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Mercury as the Jiva Lord for Taurus ascendant, as Gemini (मिथुनं जीव उच्यते) is the Jiva sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the planet governing the soul/dharma for the native with Taurus ascendant.
rules.extraction_notes: Jiva sign and its lord are identified for Taurus Lagna based on the text 'मिथुनं जीव उच्यते'.

rules.rule_name: Gemini Lagna: Deha Sign is Taurus, Lord is Venus

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a chart with Gemini (Mithuna) ascendant.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Venus as the Deha Lord for Gemini ascendant, as Taurus (वृषो देहः) is the Deha sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the planet governing the physical body/constitution for the native with Gemini ascendant.
rules.extraction_notes: Deha sign and its lord are identified for Gemini Lagna based on the text 'मिथुनस्य वृषो देहः'.

rules.rule_name: Gemini Lagna: Jiva Sign is Gemini, Lord is Mercury

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a chart with Gemini (Mithuna) ascendant.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Mercury as the Jiva Lord for Gemini ascendant, as Gemini (स्वयं जीवः) is the Jiva sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the planet governing the soul/dharma for the native with Gemini ascendant.
rules.extraction_notes: Jiva sign and its lord are identified for Gemini Lagna based on the text 'स्वयं जीवः प्रकीर्तितः'.

rules.rule_name: Cancer Lagna: Deha Sign is Cancer, Lord is Moon

check_logic.condition: When analyzing a chart with Cancer (Karka) ascendant.
check_logic.calculation: Identify Moon as the Deha Lord for Cancer ascendant, as Cancer (स्वयं देहो) is the Deha sign.

rules.impact_if_violated: Incorrect identification of the planet governing the physical body/constitution for the native with Cancer ascendant.
rules.extraction_notes: Deha sign and its lord are identified for Cancer Lagna based on the text 'कुलीरस्य स्वयं देहो'.
